name: Disable SysMain (Superfetch)
description: Stops Windows from pre-loading apps into RAM.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Analyzes app usage and pre-loads them to memory.
educationalContext.pros: Saves CPU overhead on ultra-fast NVMe drives.
educationalContext.cons: Will drastically slow down HDD loading times.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables the 'SysMain' (formerly SuperFetch) service, which preloads frequently used applications into RAM. Exact modification: 'Set-Service -Name SysMain -StartupType Disabled'. While this frees up RAM and reduces disk I/O on older HDDs, on modern NVMe SSDs the performance gain is negligible, and disabling it can actually increase application launch times as they must be read directly from the disk every time.
educationalContext.interactions: Conflicts with 'Optimize Prefetcher for SSD' if Prefetch is completely disabled. Redundant if you already have extreme RAM constraints where Windows handles paging.

name: Disable Windows Search Indexer
description: Stops constant background file scanning for search index.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Constantly reads drives in the background to build a search index.
educationalContext.pros: Saves CPU and disk I/O. Use 'Everything' app instead.
educationalContext.cons: Start menu and Explorer search will be slower.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Stops and disables the 'WSearch' service. This service continuously indexes files, emails, and other content for instant search queries within Windows Explorer and the Start Menu. Disabling it halts background CPU and disk usage significantly, but reduces Windows Search to a slow, real-time file crawl.
educationalContext.interactions: Interacts favorably with 'SetServicesManual'. If you rely on Start Menu search, disabling this will break instant results for local files.

name: Optimize System Responsiveness
description: Gives 90% CPU priority to foreground apps instead of 80%.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Changes how Windows divides CPU time between foreground (games) and background tasks.
educationalContext.pros: Reduces micro-stutters in games.
educationalContext.cons: Background tasks/downloads may be slightly slower.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile\SystemResponsiveness'. Default is 20 (meaning 20% of CPU is reserved for low-priority background tasks). Setting it to 0 or 10 gives multimedia and gaming tasks 100% access to CPU cycles.
educationalContext.interactions: Works synergistically with 'MMCSSPriority' and 'GamePriority'. Essential for high-refresh-rate gaming to eliminate micro-stutters.

name: Increase Game CPU/GPU Priority
description: Ensures games get maximum hardware scheduling priority.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Explicitly tells Windows how to prioritize processes registered as 'Games'.
educationalContext.pros: Games get higher CPU/GPU priority and scheduling preference.
educationalContext.cons: Background apps like Discord might stutter under heavy load.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Adds specific registry entries to 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile\Tasks\Games' to configure GPU Priority to 8, Priority to 6, and Scheduling Category to 'High'. This elevates the class priority of registered games under MMCSS.
educationalContext.interactions: Enhances 'DisableFSO' by ensuring the full-screen game thread is not preempted by background OS telemetry or browser threads.

execution.code: if (!(Test-Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile\Tasks\Games')) { New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile\Tasks\Games' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile\Tasks\Games' -Name 'GPU Priority' -Value 8 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile\Tasks\Games' -Name 'Priority' -Value 6 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile\Tasks\Games' -Name 'Scheduling Category' -Value 'High' -Type String -Force
execution.revertCode: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile\Tasks\Games' -Name 'GPU Priority' -Value 2 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile\Tasks\Games' -Name 'Priority' -Value 2 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile\Tasks\Games' -Name 'Scheduling Category' -Value 'Medium' -Type String -Force

name: Disable Full-Screen Optimizations
description: Allows true exclusive fullscreen mode in games by forcing GameDVR to back off.

educationalContext.howItWorks: FSO forces games into a borderless window. This tweak disables that.
educationalContext.pros: Reduces input latency by 1-5ms. Better for competitive gaming.
educationalContext.cons: Alt-Tab may be slower. Some modern DX12 games actually perform worse.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'HKCU\System\GameConfigStore\GameDVR_FSEBehaviorMode' to 2. Disables Full-Screen Optimizations, a hybrid mode introduced in Win10 where exclusive fullscreen games are rendered as borderless windowed to allow Xbox Game Bar overlays. Disabling it restores true Exclusive Fullscreen.
educationalContext.interactions: Drastically reduces input lag in older APIs (DX9/11). Interacts with 'DisableGameDVR' to fully remove the Microsoft overlay stack.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\System\GameConfigStore' -Name 'GameDVR_FSEBehaviorMode' -Value 2 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\System\GameConfigStore' -Name 'GameDVR_HonorUserFSEBehaviorMode' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\System\GameConfigStore' -Name 'GameDVR_FSEBehavior' -Value 2 -Type DWord -Force
execution.revertCode: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\System\GameConfigStore' -Name 'GameDVR_FSEBehaviorMode' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\System\GameConfigStore' -Name 'GameDVR_HonorUserFSEBehaviorMode' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\System\GameConfigStore' -Name 'GameDVR_FSEBehavior' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force

name: Disable Game DVR Background Recording
description: Stops Xbox Game Bar from recording in the background.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows constantly records the last X seconds of gameplay using GPU encoders.
educationalContext.pros: Saves 5-10% GPU performance. Eliminates random micro-stutters.
educationalContext.cons: You won't be able to use Xbox DVR 'Record that' clip sharing.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'AppCaptureEnabled' (in GameConfigStore) to 0, completely disabling the Xbox Game Bar's background recording (shadowplay equivalent). This halts the constant frame-buffer copying that inherently costs 2-5% GPU performance.
educationalContext.interactions: Redundant if Xbox features are entirely uninstalled via the Debloat tweaks. Required for 'DisableFSO' to function immaculately.

execution.code: if (!(Test-Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\GameDVR')) { New-Item -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\GameDVR' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\GameDVR' -Name 'AppCaptureEnabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; if (!(Test-Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\GameDVR')) { New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\GameDVR' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\GameDVR' -Name 'AllowGameDVR' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force

name: Reduce Telemetry to Basic
description: Minimizes diagnostic data sent to Microsoft.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets Windows data collection policy to Level 1 (Basic/Required).
educationalContext.pros: Enhances privacy and reduces background network activity.
educationalContext.cons: Microsoft gets less crash data to fix potential OS bugs.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection\AllowTelemetry' to 0 (Security level on Enterprise/Education) or 1 (Basic level on Home/Pro). Windows uses the 'DiagTrack' (Connected User Experiences and Telemetry) service to upload telemetry blobs containing usage statistics and crash dumps. This restricts the data strictly to OS version and critical error diagnostics.
educationalContext.interactions: Overrides individual telemetry toggles in the Settings app, turning them grey and displaying 'Some settings are managed by your organization'.

name: Disable Network Throttling
description: Removes bandwidth limits during multimedia playback.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows limits network to ~10Mbps while playing video/audio to prevent stuttering. This removes the limit.
educationalContext.pros: Provides maximum unrestricted download speeds.
educationalContext.cons: None for modern gigabit local networks.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile\NetworkThrottlingIndex' to FFFFFFFF. Normally, Windows throttles non-multimedia network traffic to 10 packets per millisecond to prioritize audio/video playback streams.
educationalContext.interactions: Resolves extreme ping spikes in games when another app (like Spotify or a browser tab) suddenly requests a buffer. No known downsides on modern multi-core CPUs.

name: Disable Nagle's Algorithm
description: Sends network packets immediately instead of batching.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets TcpAckFrequency and TcpNoDelay to 1 for all active network adapters.
educationalContext.pros: Reduces ping/latency in online games by preventing packet buffering.
educationalContext.cons: Slightly increases network overhead, but negligible today.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'TcpAckFrequency', 'TCPNoDelay', and 'TcpDelAckTicks' to 1 for all interfaces under 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\Interfaces\'. Nagle's algorithm buffers small packets to send them as a single larger payload, improving bandwidth efficiency but adding latency.
educationalContext.interactions: Critical for competitive gaming as it forces immediate transmission of movement/action packets. It slightly increases total packet overhead on your home router.

execution.code: $adapters = Get-NetAdapter | Where-Object {$_.Status -eq 'Up'}; foreach ($adapter in $adapters) { $tcpPath = 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\Interfaces'; Get-ChildItem -Path $tcpPath | ForEach-Object { Set-ItemProperty -Path $_.PSPath -Name 'TcpAckFrequency' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force -EA SilentlyContinue; Set-ItemProperty -Path $_.PSPath -Name 'TcpNoDelay' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force -EA SilentlyContinue } }
execution.revertCode: $tcpPath = 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\Interfaces'; Get-ChildItem -Path $tcpPath | ForEach-Object { Remove-ItemProperty -Path $_.PSPath -Name 'TcpAckFrequency' -EA SilentlyContinue; Remove-ItemProperty -Path $_.PSPath -Name 'TcpNoDelay' -EA SilentlyContinue }

name: Disable CPU Core Parking
description: Keeps all CPU cores active instead of allowing them to sleep.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Changes the hidden power plan setting CPMINCORES to 100%.
educationalContext.pros: Eliminates latency penalty from waking up sleeping cores. Great for high-end gaming.
educationalContext.cons: Significantly increases idle thermals and power draw.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies the ACPI power-management profile in 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power\PowerSettings\...\ValueMax' for Core Parking algorithms. Forces Windows to never put logical CPU cores into a deep C-state sleep.
educationalContext.interactions: Massively increases idle temperatures and power consumption. Eliminates completely the microsecond delay caused by waking up a parked core.

execution.code: powercfg -setacvalueindex scheme_current sub_processor CPMINCORES 100; powercfg -setactive scheme_current
execution.revertCode: powercfg -setacvalueindex scheme_current sub_processor CPMINCORES 5; powercfg -setactive scheme_current

name: Disable Activity History
description: Prevents Windows from tracking your app usage and browsing activity.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows collects a timeline of apps, files, and websites you use. This disables that.
educationalContext.pros: Stops Microsoft from building a timeline of your activity.
educationalContext.cons: You lose the 'Timeline' feature (press Win+Tab to see past activities).
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System\PublishUserActivities' to 0. This stops Windows from tracking which applications you open and which websites you visit (via Timeline feature) and uploading them to the cloud for cross-device synchronization.
educationalContext.interactions: Disabling this will functionally break the 'Timeline' view in Task View on older Windows 10 versions.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System' -Name 'EnableActivityFeed' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System' -Name 'PublishUserActivities' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System' -Name 'UploadUserActivities' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force
execution.revertCode: Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System' -Name 'EnableActivityFeed' -EA SilentlyContinue; Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System' -Name 'PublishUserActivities' -EA SilentlyContinue; Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System' -Name 'UploadUserActivities' -EA SilentlyContinue

name: Disable Diagnostic Data Collection
description: Stops the Connected User Experiences and Telemetry service.

educationalContext.howItWorks: The DiagTrack service sends diagnostic data to Microsoft in the background.
educationalContext.pros: Reduces CPU/network usage from background data collection.
educationalContext.cons: Microsoft receives no crash data to improve future updates.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Works alongside 'DisableTelemetry'. Specifically disables the diagnostic data viewer schedule ('DiagTrack-Listener') and clears existing diagnostic logs by triggering a 'Delete-DiagnosticData' CIM call.
educationalContext.interactions: Saves a minor amount of SSD space by preventing local telemetry JSON blobs from piling up in 'C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\Diagnosis'.

name: Disable Advertising ID
description: Prevents apps from using your advertising ID for targeted ads.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows assigns a unique advertising ID to your profile for cross-app ad targeting.
educationalContext.pros: Apps can no longer track you via advertising identifiers.
educationalContext.cons: Ads you see may be less relevant (which many consider a benefit).
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'Enabled' to 0 in 'HKCU\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AdvertisingInfo'. Disables the unique device GUID that Microsoft apps use to provide personalized retargeted advertisements across the OS.
educationalContext.interactions: Ads in Windows apps will still appear, but they will be generic rather than tracked to your personal Microsoft Account behavior.

name: Disable Location Tracking
description: Prevents Windows and apps from accessing your physical location.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Disables the Windows Location Framework that provides GPS/WiFi-based positioning to apps.
educationalContext.pros: No app or service can determine your physical location.
educationalContext.cons: Weather, Maps, Find My Device, and location-based apps will stop working.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables the 'lfsvc' (Geolocation Service) via 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services' and revokes global location app permissions in 'LetAppsAccessLocation'.
educationalContext.interactions: Breaks automated time-zone switching and accuracy of weather/maps apps. Applications will either prompt for manual zip codes or fail to geolocate.

name: Disable Clipboard Cloud Sync
description: Prevents your clipboard contents from being uploaded to Microsoft's cloud.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows can sync your clipboard across devices via your Microsoft account. This disables that.
educationalContext.pros: Sensitive copied text (passwords, keys) never leaves your device.
educationalContext.cons: You lose cross-device paste capability.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Zeroes out 'AllowCrossDeviceClipboard' in 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System'. Stops the 'cbdhsvc' (Clipboard User Service) from uploading your copied text and images (Win+V history) to Microsoft servers.
educationalContext.interactions: Does not break local clipboard history (Win+V), only the cross-device sync feature associated with your Microsoft Account.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Clipboard' -Name 'EnableClipboardHistory' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; if (!(Test-Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System')) { New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System' -Name 'AllowCrossDeviceClipboard' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force

name: Disable HPET (High Precision Timer)
description: Disables High Precision Event Timer to reduce DPC latency.

educationalContext.howItWorks: HPET is a hardware timer. On some systems it adds latency overhead vs. TSC timer.
educationalContext.pros: Can reduce DPC latency by 0.1-0.5ms, reducing micro-stutters in games.
educationalContext.cons: Some apps depend on HPET. Extremely system-specific — can cause instability.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Uses 'bcdedit /deletevalue useplatformclock' and '/set disabledynamictick yes'. Disables the High Precision Event Timer at the OS level, forcing Windows to use the invariant TSC (Time Stamp Counter). HPET reads require expensive DPC hardware polling, causing stutters.
educationalContext.interactions: Can cause system timing drift on very old platforms (Core 2 Duo era). On modern Ryzen/Core systems, disabling HPET significantly improves 1% low framerates.

execution.code: bcdedit /deletevalue useplatformclock 2>$null; bcdedit /set useplatformtick yes; bcdedit /set disabledynamictick yes

name: Disable Mouse Acceleration
description: Enables 1:1 raw mouse input by disabling pointer acceleration.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets SmoothMouseXCurve and SmoothMouseYCurve to a linear 1:1 mapping.
educationalContext.pros: Consistent aiming in FPS games — your aim matches your hand movement exactly.
educationalContext.cons: Desktop navigation may feel slower until you adjust to higher DPI settings.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Regedit 'HKCU\Control Panel\Mouse\MouseSpeed' = 0 and sets the exact curve array 'SmoothMouseXCurve'/'SmoothMouseYCurve' to linear 1:1 maps. Bypasses the 'Enhance Pointer Precision' algorithm that dynamically scales cursor speed based on physical mouse velocity.
educationalContext.interactions: Critical for raw input in older games. Modern games using Raw Input API bypass this entirely, making the tweak mostly for desktop/legacy parity.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\Control Panel\Mouse' -Name 'MouseSpeed' -Value '0' -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\Control Panel\Mouse' -Name 'MouseThreshold1' -Value '0' -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\Control Panel\Mouse' -Name 'MouseThreshold2' -Value '0' -Force
execution.revertCode: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\Control Panel\Mouse' -Name 'MouseSpeed' -Value '1' -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\Control Panel\Mouse' -Name 'MouseThreshold1' -Value '6' -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\Control Panel\Mouse' -Name 'MouseThreshold2' -Value '10' -Force

name: Enable Ultimate Performance Power Plan
description: Activates the hidden Ultimate Performance power plan for maximum throughput.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows hides a power plan designed for workstations that disables power throttling.
educationalContext.pros: Eliminates all CPU frequency scaling delays. Maximum responsiveness.
educationalContext.cons: Higher power consumption and heat generation. Not recommended for laptops.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Executes 'powercfg -duplicatescheme e9a42b02-d5df-448d-aa00-03f14749eb61'. Enables an obscured Microsoft Enterprise power plan that hides all power-saving heuristics, disables link state management, and sets CPU minimum state to 100%.
educationalContext.interactions: Overrides 'DisableCoreParking' entirely, as the Ultimate plan natively sets the parking index to 0%. Terribly destructive to laptop battery life.

name: Disable Hibernation
description: Disables hibernation and reclaims disk space from hiberfil.sys.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Hibernation writes your entire RAM to disk (hiberfil.sys). This disables it.
educationalContext.pros: Reclaims disk space equal to your RAM size (e.g. 32GB drive space saved).
educationalContext.cons: You lose the ability to hibernate. Shutdown/restart still works normally.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Runs 'powercfg.exe /hibernate off'. Deletes the 'hiberfil.sys' file from the root of the C: drive. Disables the ability for the OS to dump RAM contents to disk to enter S4 sleep state.
educationalContext.interactions: Instantly recovers physical disk space equal to 40-75% of your total RAM capacity. Also disables Fast Startup ('DisableFastStartup').

name: Optimize TCP/IP Stack
description: Tunes TCP autotuning, ECN, and RSS for maximum throughput.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Adjusts Windows TCP/IP stack parameters: enables RSS, sets autotuning to normal, enables ECN.
educationalContext.pros: Better network throughput and reduced latency on modern hardware.
educationalContext.cons: Some older routers or VPNs may not support ECN, causing connectivity issues.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Uses 'netsh int tcp set global' to apply comprehensive parameters: sets 'autotuninglevel' to normal, disables 'scalingheuristics', disables 'ecncapability', and sets 'timestamps' to disabled.
educationalContext.interactions: Disabling scalingheuristics ensures the TCP receive window isn't aggressively minimized by faulty router implementations. A bedrock baseline for network tuning.

execution.code: netsh int tcp set global autotuninglevel=normal; netsh int tcp set global ecncapability=enabled; netsh int tcp set global rss=enabled
execution.revertCode: netsh int tcp set global autotuninglevel=normal; netsh int tcp set global ecncapability=disabled; netsh int tcp set global rss=enabled

validationCmd: netsh int tcp show global | Select-String 'ECN'

name: Disable Scheduled Disk Defragmentation
description: Prevents Windows from automatically defragmenting SSDs on a schedule.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows schedules weekly TRIM/defrag operations even on SSDs.
educationalContext.pros: Saves SSD write cycles and prevents background I/O spikes.
educationalContext.cons: HDDs may fragment over time without scheduled defrag.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables the 'ScheduledDefrag' task in Task Scheduler ('\Microsoft\Windows\Defrag'). Windows periodically runs this to optimize drives (TRIM for SSDs, defrag for HDDs). Disabling it prevents background disk activity, but requires you to manually run the 'Optimize Drives' utility to maintain SSD health and performance (TRIM).
educationalContext.interactions: Can negatively impact long-term SSD performance unless you manually run TRIM or use third-party optimization tools.

name: Set Visual Effects to Performance
description: Disables all Windows UI animations and visual effects for speed.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets Windows to 'Adjust for best performance' mode, disabling transparency, animations, and shadows.
educationalContext.pros: Noticeably faster UI on older hardware. Reduced GPU/CPU load for compositing.
educationalContext.cons: The desktop looks significantly less polished — no transparency, shadows, or animations.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\VisualEffects' (VisualFXSetting=2) and disables specific UI animations in 'HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop' (e.g., UserPreferencesMask). This reduces overhead on the DWM (Desktop Window Manager) and GPU by disabling window shadows, smooth scrolling, and transparency.
educationalContext.interactions: Overrides individual UI tweaks like 'Reduce Menu Animation Delay' as it applies a blanket performance profile.

name: Clean Temporary Files
description: Deletes Windows temp files, user temp cache, and prefetch data.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Removes files from %TEMP%, Windows\Temp, and Prefetch directories.
educationalContext.pros: Reclaims disk space. Can fix some app issues caused by stale temp files.
educationalContext.cons: Apps may take slightly longer to start next time as caches are rebuilt.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Executes 'Remove-Item -Path $env:TEMP\* -Recurse -Force' and targets 'C:\Windows\Temp\*'. Unlocks and purges stranded installation residue and orphaned application caches.
educationalContext.interactions: Painless and essential. Will fail silently on files currently locked by open processes, which is the intended safe behavior.

execution.code: Remove-Item -Path "$env:TEMP\*" -Recurse -Force -EA SilentlyContinue; Remove-Item -Path "$env:SystemRoot\Temp\*" -Recurse -Force -EA SilentlyContinue; Remove-Item -Path "$env:SystemRoot\Prefetch\*" -Recurse -Force -EA SilentlyContinue
execution.revertCode: Write-Output 'Temp files cannot be restored after deletion.'

name: Flush DNS Cache
description: Clears the local DNS resolver cache to fix stale domain lookups.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Empties Windows' local DNS cache, forcing fresh lookups for all domains.
educationalContext.pros: Fixes websites that fail to load due to stale DNS records.
educationalContext.cons: Temporary slowdown as DNS cache is rebuilt over the next few minutes.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Runs 'Clear-DnsClientCache'. Immediately voids the local DNS resolver cache, forcing the OS to perform a fresh lookup on the next web request.
educationalContext.interactions: Required instantly after swapping DNS providers or attempting to bypass a stale localization node.

name: Reset Network Stack
description: Resets Winsock, IP stack, and firewall to default — fixes most network issues.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Runs netsh commands to reset the TCP/IP stack, Winsock catalog, and Windows Firewall to factory defaults.
educationalContext.pros: Fixes VPN remnants, broken adapters, and corrupt network configurations.
educationalContext.cons: Requires a reboot. You may need to reconfigure VPN/proxy settings.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Calls 'netsh winsock reset' and 'netsh int ip reset'. Wipes the layered service providers (LSPs) and TCP/IP routing tables back to factory fresh states.
educationalContext.interactions: Extremely powerful. Fixes 99% of inexplicable 'No Internet Connection' bugs. You MUST restart your PC immediately after running this.

execution.code: netsh winsock reset; netsh int ip reset; netsh advfirewall reset
execution.revertCode: Write-Output 'Network stack reset cannot be automatically reverted. Reconfigure manually.'

name: Ensure Windows Firewall is Active
description: Verifies and enables Windows Firewall on all profiles.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Enables the firewall for Domain, Private, and Public network profiles.
educationalContext.pros: Blocks unauthorized inbound traffic. Essential security baseline.
educationalContext.cons: Some peer-to-peer applications may need manual firewall rules.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Uses 'Set-NetFirewallProfile' to forcefully enable the Domain, Private, and Public firewall profiles.
educationalContext.interactions: May silently conflict with third-party antiviruses (like Kaspersky/Bitdefender) which prefer to manage the firewall themselves.

execution.revertCode: Write-Output 'Disabling Windows Firewall is not recommended.'

name: Disable Remote Desktop
description: Prevents remote connections to your PC via RDP.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Disables the Remote Desktop Protocol server listening for incoming connections.
educationalContext.pros: Closes a common attack vector used by ransomware and hackers.
educationalContext.cons: You won't be able to remotely access this PC via RDP.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'fDenyTSConnections' to 1 in 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server'. Closes Port 3389 and disables the background listening service for RDP.
educationalContext.interactions: Closes a major remote-access attack vector. You will not be able to remote into this PC until reverted.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server' -Name 'fDenyTSConnections' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force; Disable-NetFirewallRule -DisplayGroup 'Remote Desktop' -EA SilentlyContinue
execution.revertCode: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server' -Name 'fDenyTSConnections' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Enable-NetFirewallRule -DisplayGroup 'Remote Desktop' -EA SilentlyContinue

name: Disable Cortana
description: Disables Cortana assistant and prevents it from collecting data.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Cortana collects voice, typing, and search data. This policy disables it system-wide.
educationalContext.pros: Eliminates Microsoft's voice/search data collection. Reduces background CPU.
educationalContext.cons: You lose Cortana voice commands and smart search.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'AllowCortana' to 0 in 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search'. Prevents the Cortana agent from running in the background and listening for 'Hey Cortana' voice triggers. On Win11, this affects remnants of the legacy voice assistant.
educationalContext.interactions: Saves ~30-50MB of background RAM. No longer highly relevant on Windows 11 as Cortana is deprecated in favor of Copilot, but good for legacy cleanup.

execution.code: if (!(Test-Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search')) { New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search' -Name 'AllowCortana' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force

name: Disable Windows Error Reporting
description: Stops crash reports from being sent to Microsoft.

educationalContext.howItWorks: WER collects crash dumps and sends them to Microsoft for analysis.
educationalContext.pros: No crash data leaves your machine. Saves bandwidth and CPU.
educationalContext.cons: Microsoft cannot analyze crashes to improve driver/OS stability.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Error Reporting\Disabled' to 1. Stops WerFault.exe from intercepting application crashes to dump memory and send it to Microsoft.
educationalContext.interactions: Severely hampers your ability to debug BSODs or application crashes, as minidumps will no longer be reliably generated.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Error Reporting' -Name 'Disabled' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force; Stop-Service -Name 'WerSvc' -Force -EA SilentlyContinue; Set-Service -Name 'WerSvc' -StartupType Disabled
execution.revertCode: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Error Reporting' -Name 'Disabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-Service -Name 'WerSvc' -StartupType Manual

name: Disable Bing Search in Start Menu
description: Prevents Start Menu searches from querying Bing/web results.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Every Start Menu keystroke is sent to Bing. This disables web search.
educationalContext.pros: Faster local-only search. No keystrokes sent to Microsoft.
educationalContext.cons: You won't see web results in Start Menu search.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Adds 'DisableSearchBoxSuggestions' to 'HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer'. This completely disconnects the Start Menu search bar from Bing, preventing your local keystrokes from being sent to Microsoft servers while searching for local files.
educationalContext.interactions: Massively speeds up Start Menu search results and removes 'web results' clutter. Essential for privacy.

name: Disable App Suggestions & Tips
description: Stops Microsoft from suggesting apps and showing tips/ads.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows shows 'suggested apps' in Start Menu and lock screen — essentially ads.
educationalContext.pros: Removes bloatware suggestions and promotional notifications.
educationalContext.cons: You won't see Microsoft's recommended apps (most users see this as a benefit).
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'SubscribedContent-338388Enabled' and 'SubscribedContent-338389Enabled' to 0 in the ContentDeliveryManager. Disables 'suggested apps' (Candy Crush, TikTok) from silently installing or appearing in the Start Menu.
educationalContext.interactions: Prevents the OS from using network bandwidth to download bloatware payload links after major feature updates.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ContentDeliveryManager' -Name 'SystemPaneSuggestionsEnabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ContentDeliveryManager' -Name 'SilentInstalledAppsEnabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ContentDeliveryManager' -Name 'SoftLandingEnabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ContentDeliveryManager' -Name 'SubscribedContent-338388Enabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ContentDeliveryManager' -Name 'SubscribedContent-310093Enabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force

name: Disable Tailored Experiences
description: Stops Microsoft from using your diagnostic data for personalized ads.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Microsoft uses your usage data to personalize ads, tips, and recommendations.
educationalContext.pros: No personalized tracking for advertising purposes.
educationalContext.cons: Suggestions in Windows may be less relevant.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables 'Tailored Experiences with diagnostic data' in 'Privacy -> Diagnostics & feedback'. Sets 'TailoredExperiencesWithDiagnosticDataEnabled' to 0. Stops Microsoft from using telemetry data to offer tips, ads, and recommendations.
educationalContext.interactions: General privacy tightening. Works well with 'DisableActivityHistory'.

name: Disable Handwriting Data Sharing
description: Prevents Windows from sending handwriting recognition data to Microsoft.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows collects handwriting/typing patterns to improve recognition.
educationalContext.pros: Typing/handwriting patterns stay private.
educationalContext.cons: Handwriting recognition accuracy may not improve over time.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\TabletPC\PreventHandwritingDataSharing' to 1. Stops the OS from uploading your local ink strokes, typing history, and custom dictionary to Microsoft to 'improve language recognition'.
educationalContext.interactions: Mainly beneficial on 2-in-1 devices or tablets using a stylus. Redundant on desktop machines without touchscreens.

execution.code: if (!(Test-Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\TabletPC')) { New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\TabletPC' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\TabletPC' -Name 'PreventHandwritingDataSharing' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force; if (!(Test-Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\HandwritingErrorReports')) { New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\HandwritingErrorReports' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\HandwritingErrorReports' -Name 'PreventHandwritingErrorReports' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force

name: Disable App Camera Access
description: Prevents UWP/Store apps from accessing your camera.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Controls the LetAppsAccessCamera policy for Windows Store apps.
educationalContext.pros: No Store app can spy via your webcam without explicit re-enable.
educationalContext.cons: Camera won't work in UWP apps (Teams UWP, Camera app). Desktop apps unaffected.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'LetAppsAccessCamera' to 2 (Force Deny) in 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppPrivacy'. Globally blocks UWP apps from accessing the webcam.
educationalContext.interactions: May break Teams/Zoom if they use the UWP abstraction layer; however, native Win32 apps (e.g., Discord) might still bypass this. Highly destructive if you rely on video calls.

name: Disable App Microphone Access
description: Prevents UWP/Store apps from accessing your microphone.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Controls the LetAppsAccessMicrophone policy for Windows Store apps.
educationalContext.pros: No Store app can listen via your microphone without re-enabling.
educationalContext.cons: Mic won't work in UWP apps. Desktop apps (Discord, Zoom) are unaffected.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'LetAppsAccessMicrophone' to 2 (Force Deny). Functionally identical to the camera block but for audio input.
educationalContext.interactions: Breaks Voice Chat in practically all Microsoft Store games/apps. Revert this immediately if your mic 'doesn't work' in Xbox Party Chat.

name: Disable Background Apps
description: Prevents UWP apps from running in the background consuming resources.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows allows Store apps to run background tasks even when not open.
educationalContext.pros: Saves CPU, RAM, and battery. Reduces telemetry from background apps.
educationalContext.cons: UWP apps won't receive push notifications or update live tiles.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'LetAppsRunInBackground' to 2 in AppPrivacy policies. Prevents UWP (Universal Windows Platform) apps from running suspended background tasks (e.g. Mail syncing, Weather updating, Spotify hardware media keys).
educationalContext.interactions: Saves significant laptop battery life, but prevents you from receiving Mail app notifications and breaks hardware media keys for Store apps.

name: Disable Wi-Fi Sense
description: Prevents Windows from sharing Wi-Fi passwords and auto-connecting to open networks.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Wi-Fi Sense shares your saved Wi-Fi passwords with contacts via Microsoft's cloud.
educationalContext.pros: Your Wi-Fi credentials stay private and are not uploaded to Microsoft.
educationalContext.cons: You can't auto-share Wi-Fi with contacts (most consider this a benefit).
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables AutoConnectAllowedOEM in 'WcmSvc\wifinetworkmanager'. Prevents Windows from automatically connecting to open Wi-Fi hotspots and sharing network credentials with Outlook/Skype contacts.
educationalContext.interactions: Removes a major security vulnerability (credential leakage over shared networks).

name: Remove Pre-installed Bloatware
description: Removes common pre-installed Windows apps like Solitaire, News, Weather widgets, etc.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Uses Get-AppxPackage to uninstall known bloatware provisioned packages.
educationalContext.pros: Frees disk space and reduces background processes from unused apps.
educationalContext.cons: Removed apps can be reinstalled from the Microsoft Store if needed.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Uses 'Get-AppxPackage | Remove-AppxPackage' via a predefined array of known junk AppX bundle IDs (e.g., Candy Crush, Disney+, McAfee, TikTok, LinkedIn). Removes them from the current user profile.
educationalContext.interactions: Does not remove them from 'Provisioned' packages, meaning a new user account creation might still reinstall them. See provisioning tweaks for permanent nuking.

execution.code: $bloat = @('Microsoft.BingNews','Microsoft.BingWeather','Microsoft.GetHelp','Microsoft.Getstarted','Microsoft.MicrosoftSolitaireCollection','Microsoft.People','Microsoft.PowerAutomate','Microsoft.Todos','Microsoft.WindowsFeedbackHub','Microsoft.ZuneMusic','Microsoft.ZuneVideo','MicrosoftCorporationII.QuickAssist','Microsoft.WindowsMaps','Microsoft.YourPhone','Clipchamp.Clipchamp','Microsoft.MicrosoftOfficeHub','Microsoft.SkypeApp'); foreach ($app in $bloat) { Get-AppxPackage -Name $app -AllUsers -EA SilentlyContinue | Remove-AppxPackage -AllUsers -EA SilentlyContinue; Get-AppxProvisionedPackage -Online -EA SilentlyContinue | Where-Object DisplayName -eq $app | Remove-AppxProvisionedPackage -Online -EA SilentlyContinue }
execution.revertCode: Write-Output 'Removed apps can be reinstalled from the Microsoft Store.'

name: Remove OneDrive
description: Completely removes Microsoft OneDrive from the system.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Kills OneDrive, runs the uninstaller, and cleans up leftover folders/registry.
educationalContext.pros: Removes cloud sync nags and saves background CPU/network usage.
educationalContext.cons: You lose OneDrive cloud sync. Use manual backup or alternative cloud storage.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Kills the 'OneDrive.exe' process, uninstalls it using '%systemroot%\SysWOW64\OneDriveSetup.exe /uninstall', and aggressively cleans up the Explorer namespace registry keys (CLSID {018D5C66-4533-4307-9B53-224DE2ED1FE6}) removing the folder icon from the navigation pane.
educationalContext.interactions: Extremely destructive if you use OneDrive for file backups. Will break Office 365 auto-save functionality.

execution.code: Stop-Process -Name 'OneDrive' -Force -EA SilentlyContinue; Start-Sleep 2; $x86 = "$env:SystemRoot\System32\OneDriveSetup.exe"; $x64 = "$env:SystemRoot\SysWOW64\OneDriveSetup.exe"; if (Test-Path $x64) { & $x64 /uninstall } elseif (Test-Path $x86) { & $x86 /uninstall }; Remove-Item "$env:USERPROFILE\OneDrive" -Recurse -Force -EA SilentlyContinue; Remove-Item "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\OneDrive" -Recurse -Force -EA SilentlyContinue

name: Disable Xbox Services & Features
description: Disables all Xbox-related background services.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Stops and disables XboxGipSvc, XblAuthManager, XblGameSave, and XboxNetApiSvc.
educationalContext.pros: Saves CPU and RAM from Xbox services you don't use.
educationalContext.cons: Xbox Game Pass, Xbox app, and Game Bar features will stop working.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Uninstalls Xbox App, Xbox Game Bar, and disables the associated authentication and networking services (XblAuthManager, XblGameSave, XboxNetApiSvc).
educationalContext.interactions: Entirely breaks the ability to play PC Game Pass games or connect to Xbox Live multiplayer. Do not use if you are a PC Game Pass subscriber.

execution.code: $svcs = @('XboxGipSvc','XblAuthManager','XblGameSave','XboxNetApiSvc'); foreach ($s in $svcs) { Stop-Service -Name $s -Force -EA SilentlyContinue; Set-Service -Name $s -StartupType Disabled -EA SilentlyContinue }

name: Remove Cortana App
description: Removes the standalone Cortana app package from Windows.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Uninstalls the Microsoft.549981C3F5F10 (Cortana) app package.
educationalContext.pros: Fully removes Cortana UWP app and its background processes.
educationalContext.cons: Cortana voice commands become unavailable (policy disable in Privacy is separate).
educationalContext.expertDetails: Forcefully uninstalls the 'Microsoft.549981C3F5F10' (Cortana) AppX package. This is a step further than just disabling the background service.
educationalContext.interactions: Completely removes the Cortana icon from the Start Menu. Updates to Windows 11 normally handle this automatically now.

execution.code: Get-AppxPackage -Name 'Microsoft.549981C3F5F10' -AllUsers -EA SilentlyContinue | Remove-AppxPackage -AllUsers -EA SilentlyContinue; Get-AppxProvisionedPackage -Online -EA SilentlyContinue | Where-Object DisplayName -eq 'Microsoft.549981C3F5F10' | Remove-AppxProvisionedPackage -Online -EA SilentlyContinue
execution.revertCode: Write-Output 'Reinstall Cortana from the Microsoft Store.'

name: Remove 3D Viewer & Mixed Reality
description: Removes 3D Viewer, 3D Builder, Paint 3D, and Mixed Reality Portal.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Uninstalls 3D/MR packages that most users never open.
educationalContext.pros: Reclaims disk space from unused 3D applications.
educationalContext.cons: You lose 3D model viewing capabilities (rarely needed).
educationalContext.expertDetails: Uninstalls the 'MixedReality.Portal', '3DBuilder', and 'Print3D' AppX packages, and removes the '3D Objects' folder from 'This PC' in File Explorer by deleting its namespace CLSID.
educationalContext.interactions: Cleans up File Explorer significantly. No downsides unless you own a Windows Mixed Reality headset.

execution.code: $apps = @('Microsoft.Microsoft3DViewer','Microsoft.3DBuilder','Microsoft.MSPaint','Microsoft.MixedReality.Portal','Microsoft.Print3D'); foreach ($app in $apps) { Get-AppxPackage -Name $app -AllUsers -EA SilentlyContinue | Remove-AppxPackage -AllUsers -EA SilentlyContinue }
execution.revertCode: Write-Output 'Reinstall removed apps from the Microsoft Store.'

name: Disable Widgets Board (Win 11)
description: Disables the Windows 11 Widgets panel and its background process.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets TaskbarDa policy to 0, preventing the Widgets board from loading.
educationalContext.pros: Saves RAM and CPU from the Edge WebView2 process that powers Widgets.
educationalContext.cons: You lose the news/weather/stocks widget panel on the taskbar.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables the 'TaskbarDa' feature (Windows 11 Widgets board) via Group Policy ('Dsh\AllowNewsAndInterests'). Kills the associated 'msedgewebview2' processes that render the widgets.
educationalContext.interactions: Frees up ~150-300MB of RAM and removes the distracting weather icon/news feed from the left side of the Windows 11 taskbar.

name: Disable Microsoft Teams Autostart
description: Prevents Microsoft Teams from launching at startup.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Disables the Teams Chat icon and removes startup registry entries.
educationalContext.pros: Faster boot times and less background RAM usage.
educationalContext.cons: Teams won't auto-connect on login. You can still launch it manually.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Removes the 'Teams' string from 'HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run' and disables the built-in Windows 11 'Chat' icon from the taskbar ('TaskbarMn').
educationalContext.interactions: Speeds up login times heavily. You can still manually open Teams, but it won't force-load into the system tray on boot.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced' -Name 'TaskbarMn' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; $startupPath = 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run'; Remove-ItemProperty -Path $startupPath -Name 'com.squirrel.Teams.Teams' -EA SilentlyContinue; Remove-ItemProperty -Path $startupPath -Name 'MicrosoftTeams' -EA SilentlyContinue

name: Disable Windows Copilot
description: Disables Windows Copilot AI assistant and removes its taskbar button.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets TurnOffWindowsCopilot policy and removes the taskbar Copilot button.
educationalContext.pros: No AI sidebar. Saves Edge WebView2 RAM and prevents data collection.
educationalContext.cons: You lose the Copilot AI assistant in Windows.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'TurnOffWindowsCopilot' to 1 in 'HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsCopilot'. This disables the centralized AI assistant, completely removing the Copilot icon from the taskbar and Win+C shortcut.
educationalContext.interactions: Removes aggressive AI integrations. Win+C will no longer do anything.

execution.code: if (!(Test-Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsCopilot')) { New-Item -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsCopilot' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsCopilot' -Name 'TurnOffWindowsCopilot' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced' -Name 'ShowCopilotButton' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force

name: Restore Classic Right-Click Menu (Win 11)
description: Brings back the full Windows 10 right-click context menu in Windows 11.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Creates an InprocServer32 override that forces Explorer to use the legacy context menu.
educationalContext.pros: All right-click options visible immediately without clicking 'Show more options'.
educationalContext.cons: Requires Explorer restart. New Windows 11 context menu extensions won't appear.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Creates an empty key at 'HKCU\Software\Classes\CLSID\{86ca1aa0-34aa-4e8b-a509-50c905bae2a2}\InprocServer32'. This overrides the COM object for the Windows 11 XAML context menu, forcing Explorer back to the legacy Win32 command bar system.
educationalContext.interactions: Eliminates the 'Show More Options' barrier in Windows 11. Requires an explorer.exe restart to take effect.

name: Show File Extensions
description: Shows file extensions (.exe, .pdf, .docx) for all files.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets HideFileExt to 0 so Windows displays file extensions in Explorer.
educationalContext.pros: See exactly what type every file is. Helps identify disguised malware (.pdf.exe).
educationalContext.cons: File names look slightly longer. Renaming requires preserving the extension.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Changes 'HideFileExt' in 'HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced' to 0. Forces Explorer to display the raw extension (e.g., .txt, .exe) instead of hiding it based on file association.
educationalContext.interactions: Crucial anti-malware step preventing 'document.pdf.exe' spoofing attacks.

name: Show Hidden Files & Folders
description: Makes hidden files, folders, and system files visible in Explorer.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets Hidden to 1 and ShowSuperHidden to 1 in Explorer Advanced settings.
educationalContext.pros: See all files including AppData, hidden configs, and system files.
educationalContext.cons: Explorer shows many system files you normally wouldn't interact with.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Changes 'Hidden' in 'Explorer\Advanced' to 1. Forces the display of files and folders marked with the 'Hidden' file attribute.
educationalContext.interactions: Your user directory will now show 'AppData', and drives will show '$RECYCLE.BIN'.

name: Align Taskbar to Left (Win 11)
description: Moves the Windows 11 taskbar icons to the left instead of center.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets TaskbarAl registry value to 0 (left-aligned).
educationalContext.pros: Traditional left-aligned taskbar like Windows 10.
educationalContext.cons: Purely cosmetic preference — no performance impact.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'Al' in 'HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced' to 0 (Left). Centers the Start button exactly like Windows 10.
educationalContext.interactions: Purely ergonomic. Does not affect the ability to use third party tools like StartAllBack, which heavily override this.

name: Enable Verbose Startup Messages
description: Shows detailed status messages during boot, shutdown, logon, and logoff.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets VerboseStatus in the Windows policies to show step-by-step boot messages.
educationalContext.pros: Helps diagnose slow boot by showing exactly what's happening.
educationalContext.cons: Boot screen shows text instead of the animation spinner.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Adds 'VerboseStatus' (DWORD 1) to 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'. Replaces the spinning dots on boot/shutdown with highly detailed text explaining exactly what Windows is doing (e.g., 'Applying computer policy', 'Starting Group Policy Services').
educationalContext.interactions: Invaluable for troubleshooting slow boot times or stalled shutdown operations.

name: Disable Delivery Optimization (P2P Updates)
description: Prevents Windows from uploading updates to other PCs on the internet.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows shares downloaded updates with other PCs via peer-to-peer, using your bandwidth.
educationalContext.pros: Your upload bandwidth is never used to distribute Microsoft updates.
educationalContext.cons: Updates may download slightly slower without peer sources.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Translates to setting 'DODownloadMode' to 0 in 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DeliveryOptimization'. Stops your PC from uploading Windows Updates and Store apps to other PCs on your local network or the internet (P2P).
educationalContext.interactions: Saves massive amounts of upload bandwidth on home connections. You must reboot for this to apply cleanly to the DOsvc.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\DeliveryOptimization\Config' -Name 'DODownloadMode' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force -EA SilentlyContinue; if (!(Test-Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DeliveryOptimization')) { New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DeliveryOptimization' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DeliveryOptimization' -Name 'DODownloadMode' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force

name: Disable Auto-Restart After Updates
description: Prevents Windows from automatically restarting your PC after installing updates.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets NoAutoRebootWithLoggedOnUsers policy so Windows waits for you to restart manually.
educationalContext.pros: Never lose unsaved work from surprise restarts.
educationalContext.cons: You must remember to restart after updates to apply security patches.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'NoAutoRebootWithLoggedOnUsers' to 1. Prevents Windows Update from forcibly restarting your PC overnight or while you are rendering a video.
educationalContext.interactions: You must remember to manually restart your PC after updates install, or they will stay pending.

name: Disable Driver Updates via Windows Update
description: Prevents Windows Update from installing driver updates automatically.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets ExcludeWUDriversInQualityUpdate policy to block driver delivery via WU.
educationalContext.pros: Prevents broken drivers from being force-installed. Essential for GPU users.
educationalContext.cons: You must manually update drivers via manufacturer tools (NVIDIA, AMD, Intel).
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'ExcludeWUDriversInQualityUpdate' to 1. Strongly instructs Windows Update to never automatically download and overwrite your cleanly installed GPU, Audio, or Chipset drivers.
educationalContext.interactions: Essential for gamers to prevent Windows from randomly overwriting a stable proprietary NVIDIA/AMD driver with a broken DCH baseline driver.

name: Pause Windows Updates for 35 Days
description: Pauses all Windows updates for the maximum allowed 35-day period.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets the FlightSettingsMaxPauseDays and PauseUpdatesStartTime in Windows Update settings.
educationalContext.pros: No updates interrupt your workflow for over a month.
educationalContext.cons: You miss security patches during the pause. Re-apply after 35 days.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Writes a futuristic date into 'PauseUpdatesExpiryTime'. Instantly pauses all Windows Update activity for roughly 35 days, bypassing the restrictive 7-day GUI limit.
educationalContext.interactions: You will miss out on zero-day security patches. Must be clicked again every month, or updates will resume with a vengeance.

execution.revertCode: Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\WindowsUpdate\UX\Settings' -Name 'PauseUpdatesExpiryTime' -EA SilentlyContinue; Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\WindowsUpdate\UX\Settings' -Name 'PauseQualityUpdatesStartTime' -EA SilentlyContinue; Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\WindowsUpdate\UX\Settings' -Name 'PauseFeatureUpdatesStartTime' -EA SilentlyContinue

name: Security-Only Updates (Delay Feature Updates)
description: Defers feature updates by 365 days, allowing only security patches.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets DeferFeatureUpdatesPeriodInDays to 365 via Group Policy.
educationalContext.pros: You get security fixes but avoid potentially unstable feature updates for a year.
educationalContext.cons: You'll be on an older Windows version. Some new features won't be available.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'BranchReadinessLevel' and 'DeferFeatureUpdatesPeriodInDays' via advanced Windows Update group policies. Forces the system to only ingest critical security fixes (Quality Updates) while delaying UI/Feature bloated updates by 365 days.
educationalContext.interactions: The golden standard for operational stability. Keeps you safe without forcing new broken features down your throat.

execution.code: if (!(Test-Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsUpdate')) { New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsUpdate' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsUpdate' -Name 'DeferFeatureUpdates' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsUpdate' -Name 'DeferFeatureUpdatesPeriodInDays' -Value 365 -Type DWord -Force

name: Disable SMBv1 Protocol
description: Disables the legacy SMBv1 protocol exploited by WannaCry and other ransomware.

educationalContext.howItWorks: SMBv1 is a 30-year-old file sharing protocol with known critical vulnerabilities.
educationalContext.pros: Eliminates the attack vector used by WannaCry, NotPetya, and EternalBlue.
educationalContext.cons: Very old NAS devices or XP-era machines may not connect for file sharing.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'SMB1' to 0 in 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanServer\Parameters' and uninstalls the 'FS-SMB1' optional feature.
educationalContext.interactions: Closes the attack vector used by WannaCry ransomware. Do not revert this unless accessing a 20-year-old network drive.

name: Disable Windows Script Host
description: Prevents .vbs and .js scripts from running — blocks a common malware vector.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Disables the Windows Script Host engine (wscript.exe / cscript.exe).
educationalContext.pros: Blocks VBScript/JScript malware that arrives via email attachments.
educationalContext.cons: Legitimate .vbs scripts (login scripts, admin tools) won't execute.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'Enabled' to 0 in 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows Script Host\Settings'. Prevents execution of .vbs and .js files natively by wscript.exe or cscript.exe.
educationalContext.interactions: An excellent defense against email phishing payloads. May break very old legacy login scripts in enterprise active directories.

name: Disable Lock Screen Ads & Tips
description: Removes Microsoft ads, tips, and 'fun facts' from the lock screen.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Disables RotatingLockScreenOverlayEnabled and related ContentDeliveryManager keys.
educationalContext.pros: Clean lock screen without promotional content.
educationalContext.cons: You won't see Windows tips or Bing daily images on the lock screen.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ContentDeliveryManager' -Name 'RotatingLockScreenOverlayEnabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ContentDeliveryManager' -Name 'RotatingLockScreenEnabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ContentDeliveryManager' -Name 'SubscribedContent-338387Enabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force

name: Disable AutoPlay & AutoRun
description: Prevents USB drives and CDs from automatically running programs when inserted.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Disables AutoPlay for all media types, blocking autorun.inf execution.
educationalContext.pros: Blocks a major USB malware infection vector.
educationalContext.cons: You must manually open USB drives in Explorer instead of auto-launching.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'NoDriveTypeAutoRun' to 255 (FF). Completely disables Windows from automatically executing 'autorun.inf' or prompting what to do when inserting a USB drive or CD.
educationalContext.interactions: Prevents automated malware infections (BadUSB/Autorun worms) from executing merely by plugging in a flash drive.

name: Disable Spectre/Meltdown Mitigations
description: Disables CPU vulnerability mitigations for maximum performance at the cost of security.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Removes kernel-level Spectre V2 and Meltdown patches that add CPU overhead.
educationalContext.pros: Can recover 5-30% CPU performance depending on workload and CPU generation.
educationalContext.cons: Your system is vulnerable to Spectre/Meltdown side-channel attacks. DANGEROUS.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'FeatureSettingsOverride' to 3 and 'FeatureSettingsOverrideMask' to 3. Disables the Retpoline and kernel-page-table isolation (KPTI) patches for hardware-level CPU exploits (Meltdown/Spectre).
educationalContext.interactions: Increases I/O and syscall performance heavily on older Intel architectures (Haswell/Skylake). Extremely dangerous if you run untrusted virtual machines or execute random JavaScript.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Memory Management' -Name 'FeatureSettingsOverride' -Value 3 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Memory Management' -Name 'FeatureSettingsOverrideMask' -Value 3 -Type DWord -Force
execution.revertCode: Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Memory Management' -Name 'FeatureSettingsOverride' -EA SilentlyContinue; Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Memory Management' -Name 'FeatureSettingsOverrideMask' -EA SilentlyContinue

name: Remove Startup App Delay
description: Removes the artificial 10-second delay Windows adds before launching startup apps.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows adds a ~10 second delay after logon before running startup programs.
educationalContext.pros: Startup apps launch immediately after login, faster overall boot experience.
educationalContext.cons: If too many apps start at once, there may be brief system load.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Creates or modifies 'HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Serialize' -> 'StartupDelayInMSec' to 0. By default, Windows delays the launch of startup apps by ~10 seconds to prioritize loading the Desktop and core services. Setting this to 0 forces all startup apps to launch immediately.
educationalContext.interactions: May cause the desktop to feel unresponsive immediately after login if you have many startup apps enabled. Pairs well with 'Clean Temporary Files' and debloat tweaks.

name: Disable NTFS Last Access Timestamp
description: Stops NTFS from updating the 'last accessed' timestamp on every file read.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Every file read writes a timestamp back to disk. This disables that overhead.
educationalContext.pros: Reduces SSD/HDD writes significantly, especially for file-heavy workloads.
educationalContext.cons: Forensic tools and some backup software rely on last access timestamps.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Executes 'fsutil behavior set disablelastaccess 1'. This stops the NTFS file system from updating the 'Last Accessed' timestamp attribute of files and directories every time they are opened. This reduces micro-writes to the storage drive, slightly improving I/O performance and increasing SSD lifespan.
educationalContext.interactions: Generally safe, but may break specific forensic or backup tools that rely on the 'Last Accessed' metadata to determine file usage.

name: Disable 8.3 Short File Names
description: Disables legacy DOS 8.3 filename generation on NTFS volumes.

educationalContext.howItWorks: NTFS creates a short 8.3 alias (like PROGRA~1) for every file, adding overhead.
educationalContext.pros: Speeds up file creation in directories with many files (10-20% improvement).
educationalContext.cons: Very old 16-bit applications may fail to find files.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Executes 'fsutil behavior set disable8dot3 1'. Disables the legacy NTFS feature that automatically generates 8.3 character (DOS-compatible) short names for all long file names. This significantly speeds up directory enumeration in folders with thousands of files.
educationalContext.interactions: Breaking risk: 16-bit applications or extremely old enterprise software hardcoded to use MS-DOS paths (like 'PROGRA~1') will fail.

name: Disable Print Spooler Service
description: Stops the Print Spooler service — saves resources and closes the PrintNightmare vector.

educationalContext.howItWorks: The Print Spooler runs even if you have no printer. It's been a recurring CVE target.
educationalContext.pros: Reduces attack surface (PrintNightmare) and saves ~20MB RAM.
educationalContext.cons: You cannot print to any printer while this service is disabled.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables the 'Spooler' service. It is responsible for managing print jobs, loading printer drivers, and handling the print queue. Disabling it frees up a small amount of RAM and closes common attack vectors (like PrintNightmare).
educationalContext.interactions: Prevents all printing, including PDF printers ('Print to PDF'). Must be reverted if you ever need to connect a physical or virtual printer.

name: Disable Fax Service
description: Disables the Windows Fax service that nobody uses in the modern era.

educationalContext.howItWorks: The Fax service sits idle consuming memory, waiting for fax transmissions.
educationalContext.pros: Saves resources from an obsolete service.
educationalContext.cons: You can't send faxes (you weren't going to).
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables the 'Fax' service. This legacy component allows the computer to send and receive faxes. It runs silently in the background but offers zero value to modern users.
educationalContext.interactions: Completely isolated tweak. No negative interactions unless you are using a physical fax modem.

name: Disable App Contacts Access
description: Prevents apps from accessing your contacts list.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Denies UWP apps access to the Contacts capability via ConsentStore.
educationalContext.pros: No app can read your contact list without re-enabling.
educationalContext.cons: People/Mail apps won't auto-complete contact names.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Globally revokes App Access to the Windows Contacts database ('LetAppsAccessContacts').
educationalContext.interactions: Breaks autocomplete in the native Mail and People apps.

name: Disable App Calendar Access
description: Prevents apps from reading your calendar appointments.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Denies UWP apps access to the Calendar capability.
educationalContext.pros: Calendar data stays private from third-party apps.
educationalContext.cons: Calendar sync apps won't function properly.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Globally revokes App Access to the Calendar.
educationalContext.interactions: Breaks the Taskbar calendar flyout agenda view and native Calendar app syncing.

name: Disable App Email Access
description: Prevents apps from reading your email.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Denies UWP apps access to the Email capability.
educationalContext.pros: No app can silently read your email content.
educationalContext.cons: Windows Mail app won't function.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Prevents UWP apps from accessing locally stored email databases.
educationalContext.interactions: Renders the native Windows Mail app completely inoperable until reverted.

name: Disable App Messaging Access
description: Prevents apps from reading or sending SMS/MMS messages.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Denies UWP apps access to the Chat/Messaging capability.
educationalContext.pros: No app can read your text messages.
educationalContext.cons: Your Phone Companion messaging won't work.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Prevents apps from reading or sending SMS/MMS messages (usually via Phone Link).
educationalContext.interactions: Breaks the messaging capabilities of the 'Phone Link' app.

name: Disable App Notification Access
description: Prevents apps from accessing your notification history.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Denies UWP apps access to user notifications.
educationalContext.pros: No app can read your notifications from other apps.
educationalContext.cons: Some apps that aggregate notifications won't function.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Prevents apps from reading other apps' notifications.
educationalContext.interactions: Enhances privacy against malicious apps scraping notification contents (like OTPs).

name: Disable App Account Info Access
description: Prevents apps from accessing your Microsoft account name and picture.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Denies UWP apps access to your account information.
educationalContext.pros: Apps can't see your name, email, or profile picture.
educationalContext.cons: Some personalized app experiences won't work.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'LetAppsAccessAccountInfo' to 2. Stops apps from reading your Microsoft Account name, picture, and domain details.
educationalContext.interactions: Some games downloaded from the Xbox App may fail to authenticate seamlessly if they cannot read your Xbox Live gamertag info.

name: Disable Online Speech Recognition
description: Prevents Windows from sending your voice data to Microsoft's cloud for processing.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Disables the cloud-based speech recognition that sends voice to Microsoft servers.
educationalContext.pros: Your voice data stays on your device. Offline recognition still works.
educationalContext.cons: Speech recognition quality may be lower without cloud processing.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables the cloud-based speech dictation service (Win+H) which sends your voice to Microsoft for processing.
educationalContext.interactions: Breaks the Win+H dictation overlay completely. Local accessibility speech recognition remains unaffected.

name: Disable Input Personalization
description: Stops Windows from learning your typing patterns and building a user dictionary.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows learns your writing style to improve autocomplete. This data can be shared.
educationalContext.pros: Your typing patterns and vocabulary are not collected.
educationalContext.cons: Autocomplete and text predictions won't improve over time.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Clears the local custom dictionary and stops Windows from locally analyzing your typing patterns to predict text.
educationalContext.interactions: You will lose autocorrect and text prediction across native Windows text fields.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\InputPersonalization' -Name 'RestrictImplicitInkCollection' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\InputPersonalization' -Name 'RestrictImplicitTextCollection' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\InputPersonalization\TrainedDataStore' -Name 'HarvestContacts' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Personalization\Settings' -Name 'AcceptedPrivacyPolicy' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force

name: Disable Feedback Notifications
description: Stops Windows from asking for feedback and sending usage frequency data.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets feedback frequency to Never and disables the Feedback Hub prompts.
educationalContext.pros: No more 'Rate your experience' popups or background feedback collection.
educationalContext.cons: Microsoft receives less direct user feedback.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables the 'Windows Feedback Experience' service and associated scheduled tasks that periodically prompt 'How likely are you to recommend Windows 10?'.
educationalContext.interactions: Removes a major annoyance with zero drawbacks.

execution.code: if (!(Test-Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Siuf\Rules')) { New-Item -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Siuf\Rules' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Siuf\Rules' -Name 'NumberOfSIUFInPeriod' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Siuf\Rules' -Name 'PeriodInNanoSeconds' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force

name: Disable Automatic Map Updates
description: Prevents Windows from automatically downloading map data in the background.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Disables the MapsBroker service and auto-update settings.
educationalContext.pros: Saves bandwidth and disk space from map data downloads.
educationalContext.cons: Windows Maps app won't have up-to-date offline maps.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables the 'MapsBroker' service out of 'MapsUpdateTask', stopping background downloads of offline map data.
educationalContext.interactions: No negative effects unless you actively use the Windows Maps app offline.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\Maps' -Name 'AutoUpdateEnabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Stop-Service -Name 'MapsBroker' -Force -EA SilentlyContinue; Set-Service -Name 'MapsBroker' -StartupType Disabled -EA SilentlyContinue

name: Disable Defender Auto Sample Submission
description: Prevents Windows Defender from automatically uploading suspicious files to Microsoft.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Defender uploads files it considers suspicious to Microsoft's cloud for analysis.
educationalContext.pros: Your files never leave your PC. Essential for privacy-sensitive work.
educationalContext.cons: New malware detection may be slightly slower without cloud analysis.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'SubmitSamplesConsent' to 2 (Never Send) in 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows Defender\Spynet'.
educationalContext.interactions: Prevents Defender from uploading unknown executing files directly from your SSD to Microsoft servers for cloud analysis.

name: Disable SmartScreen Filter
description: Disables the SmartScreen filter for apps and web browsing.

educationalContext.howItWorks: SmartScreen checks downloaded files and URLs against Microsoft's database.
educationalContext.pros: No data about your downloads sent to Microsoft. Faster first-run of new apps.
educationalContext.cons: You lose protection against known malicious downloads. USE WITH CAUTION.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'EnableSmartScreen' to 0 and 'SmartScreenEnabled' to 'Off' across Explorer and Edge paradigms.
educationalContext.interactions: Stops Windows from sending hashes of every downloaded executable to Microsoft for reputation checking. Removes the "Windows protected your PC" blue popup.

name: Disable Microsoft Edge Telemetry
description: Disables Edge browser data collection and telemetry services.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Disables Edge's MicrosoftEdgeUpdate and telemetry scheduled tasks.
educationalContext.pros: Stops Edge from phoning home when you don't use it as your browser.
educationalContext.cons: Edge may not auto-update (use Windows Update instead).
educationalContext.expertDetails: Adds policies to 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Edge' (SendSiteInfoToImproveServices, MetricsReportingEnabled) to stop the Edge browser from uploading browsing history and interaction telemetry to MS.
educationalContext.interactions: Works regardless of whether Edge is your primary browser, as webview2 instances are used across the OS.

execution.code: if (!(Test-Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Edge')) { New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Edge' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Edge' -Name 'MetricsReportingEnabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Edge' -Name 'SendSiteInfoToImproveServices' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Edge' -Name 'PersonalizationReportingEnabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force

name: Disable Telemetry Scheduled Tasks
description: Disables Windows scheduled tasks that collect and submit telemetry data.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Disables Microsoft Compatibility Appraiser, ProgramDataUpdater, Proxy, and other telemetry tasks.
educationalContext.pros: Eliminates periodic background telemetry collection that spikes CPU usage.
educationalContext.cons: Windows compatibility checks and diagnostic data won't run.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Uses 'schtasks /change /disable' on various deeply embedded Microsoft scheduled tasks under '\Microsoft\Windows\Customer Experience Improvement Program'. Stops the OS from waking up to compress and send telemetry logs.
educationalContext.interactions: Eliminates highly irritating 3 AM CPU/Disk usage spikes common on Windows 10/11.

execution.code: $tasks = @('\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\Microsoft Compatibility Appraiser','\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\ProgramDataUpdater','\Microsoft\Windows\Autochk\Proxy','\Microsoft\Windows\Customer Experience Improvement Program\Consolidator','\Microsoft\Windows\Customer Experience Improvement Program\UsbCeip','\Microsoft\Windows\DiskDiagnostic\Microsoft-Windows-DiskDiagnosticDataCollector'); foreach ($t in $tasks) { schtasks /Change /TN $t /Disable 2>$null }
execution.revertCode: $tasks = @('\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\Microsoft Compatibility Appraiser','\Microsoft\Windows\Application Experience\ProgramDataUpdater','\Microsoft\Windows\Autochk\Proxy','\Microsoft\Windows\Customer Experience Improvement Program\Consolidator','\Microsoft\Windows\Customer Experience Improvement Program\UsbCeip'); foreach ($t in $tasks) { schtasks /Change /TN $t /Enable 2>$null }

name: Disable Settings Sync
description: Prevents Windows from syncing your settings, passwords, and themes to Microsoft's cloud.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows syncs theme, password, language, and other settings across devices.
educationalContext.pros: Your settings and saved passwords never leave your device.
educationalContext.cons: Settings won't sync to other Windows PCs using your Microsoft account.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables 'Sync your settings' via group policy. Stops Windows from uploading your wallpaper, theme, passwords, and language preferences to the cloud.
educationalContext.interactions: Prevents new Windows installations from automatically pulling down your previous desktop wallpaper and Wi-Fi passwords.
