execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\SettingSync' -Name 'SyncPolicy' -Value 5 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\SettingSync\Groups\Personalization' -Name 'Enabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force -EA SilentlyContinue; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\SettingSync\Groups\BrowserSettings' -Name 'Enabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force -EA SilentlyContinue; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\SettingSync\Groups\Credentials' -Name 'Enabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force -EA SilentlyContinue

name: Set DNS to Cloudflare (1.1.1.1)
description: Sets your primary DNS to Cloudflare's 1.1.1.1 and Google's 8.8.8.8 as backup.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Replaces your ISP's DNS with faster, privacy-focused DNS resolvers.
educationalContext.pros: Faster resolution, no ISP DNS logging, blocks some phishing domains.
educationalContext.cons: If Cloudflare is down, falls back to Google DNS.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets primary DNS to 1.1.1.1 and secondary to 1.0.0.1. Directly interfaces with Cloudflare's massive global CDN network for the lowest latency possible.
educationalContext.interactions: Overrides your ISP's default DHCP-assigned DNS.

name: Enable Storage Sense Auto Cleanup
description: Enables automatic cleanup of temp files, recycle bin, and downloads when disk is low.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Storage Sense auto-cleans temp files every 30 days and empties Recycle Bin.
educationalContext.pros: Automatically frees disk space without manual intervention.
educationalContext.cons: Files in Downloads older than 30 days may be deleted (configurable).
educationalContext.expertDetails: Enables the 'StoragePolicy' keys to automatically run Storage Sense. Instructs Windows to silently clean up recycle bin files older than 30 days and purge the downloads folder dynamically.
educationalContext.interactions: A set-and-forget maintenance routine. Can be disastrous if you treat your Downloads folder as a permanent archive.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\StorageSense\Parameters\StoragePolicy' -Name '01' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\StorageSense\Parameters\StoragePolicy' -Name '04' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\StorageSense\Parameters\StoragePolicy' -Name '08' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\StorageSense\Parameters\StoragePolicy' -Name '2048' -Value 30 -Type DWord -Force

name: Clean Windows Component Store
description: Runs DISM to clean up superseded Windows update packages and free disk space.

educationalContext.howItWorks: DISM removes old component store versions that are no longer needed.
educationalContext.pros: Can reclaim 1-5GB of disk space from accumulated update leftovers.
educationalContext.cons: You cannot uninstall old updates after cleanup. Takes 2-5 minutes.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Runs 'DISM.exe /Online /Cleanup-Image /StartComponentCleanup /ResetBase'. Brutally compresses the WinSxS folder by deleting superseded versions of Windows Updates and permanently baking in the current updates.
educationalContext.interactions: Frees up to 5-10GB of SSD space space instantly. The huge downside is that you can NEVER uninstall current Windows Updates once this processes—they become permanent.

execution.revertCode: Write-Output 'Component store cleanup cannot be reversed.'

name: Disable App Radio Control Access
description: Prevents apps from controlling your Bluetooth and Wi-Fi radios.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Denies UWP apps access to toggle radios (Bluetooth, Wi-Fi) on/off.
educationalContext.pros: No app can silently enable Bluetooth or Wi-Fi.
educationalContext.cons: Some companion apps may not toggle Bluetooth devices.

name: Disable Meet Now Taskbar Button
description: Removes the 'Meet Now' (Skype) icon from the taskbar system tray.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets HideSCAMeetNow policy to remove the Meet Now camera button.
educationalContext.pros: Cleaner taskbar. Removes unused Skype integration.
educationalContext.cons: You lose quick access to Skype Meet Now.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Hides the 'Meet Now' (Skype integration) button from the Windows 10 system tray via 'HideSCAMeetNow' registry DWORD.
educationalContext.interactions: Purely cosmetic UI cleanup.

name: Enable Hardware GPU Scheduling
description: Enables hardware-accelerated GPU scheduling for lower input latency.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Moves GPU memory management from the CPU to the GPU's own scheduling processor.
educationalContext.pros: Reduces input latency by 1-3ms. Less CPU overhead for GPU operations.
educationalContext.cons: Requires WDDM 2.7+ GPU drivers (NVIDIA 10xx/RTX, AMD RX 5000+). May cause instability on older GPUs.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\GraphicsDrivers\HwSchMode' = 2. Offloads high-frequency graphics scheduling from the CPU to a dedicated hardware processor on the GPU. Reduces CPU overhead in heavily draw-call bound scenarios.
educationalContext.interactions: Requires a reboot and WDDM 2.7+ capable drivers. Can cause stability issues or VR stuttering on specific NVIDIA/AMD driver revisions.

name: Disable CPU Power Throttling
description: Prevents Windows from throttling CPU performance for background efficiency.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows 10+ uses Power Throttling to slow down background apps. This disables it entirely.
educationalContext.pros: All processes run at full CPU speed. Eliminates frame drops from throttled threads.
educationalContext.cons: Higher power consumption and heat. Not recommended for laptops on battery.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power\PowerThrottling\PowerThrottlingOff' = 1. Prevents Windows 10/11 from aggressively putting background processes into EcoQoS (Efficiency mode), which limits them to E-cores or lowers their clock speed.
educationalContext.interactions: Increases battery drain on laptops significantly. Counteracts the OS scheduler's attempt to isolate intensive games from background polling.

name: Set CSRSS to High Priority
description: Sets the Windows Client/Server Runtime to high priority for smoother input processing.

educationalContext.howItWorks: CSRSS handles raw input (mouse/keyboard). Higher priority = faster input processing.
educationalContext.pros: Measurably reduces mouse input latency in competitive games.
educationalContext.cons: Other system processes get less CPU time. Can cause instability on weak CPUs.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Changes the CPUPriorityClass of the Client Server Runtime Process (csrss.exe) from normal to High via 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\csrss.exe'. This prioritizes hardware interrupt and Win32 subsystem handling.
educationalContext.interactions: Can slightly decrease input latency. Incorrectly setting this to 'Realtime' (Hex 3) instead of 'High' (Hex 4) would cause catastrophic system freezes.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\csrss.exe\PerfOptions' -Name 'CpuPriorityClass' -Value 4 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\csrss.exe\PerfOptions' -Name 'IoPriority' -Value 3 -Type DWord -Force
execution.revertCode: Remove-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\csrss.exe' -Recurse -Force -EA SilentlyContinue

name: Disable Windows Game Mode
description: Disables Windows Game Mode which can paradoxically cause stuttering in some games.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Game Mode is supposed to prioritize games, but its background thread management often causes stutters.
educationalContext.pros: Eliminates Game Mode-induced micro-stutters and frame time inconsistencies.
educationalContext.cons: You lose Game Mode's automatic background app suppression.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables Windows 'Game Mode' ('HKCU\Software\Microsoft\GameBar\AllowAutoGameMode'). Historically, Game Mode caused massive stuttering by overly aggressively locking out background processes (like OBS or Discord).
educationalContext.interactions: In newer Windows 11 builds, Game Mode is generally stable and beneficial. Disabling it is now mostly for troubleshooting encoder streams (e.g. OBS NVENC dropframes).

name: Optimize Prefetcher for SSD
description: Sets Prefetcher to application-launch-only mode optimized for SSDs.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows Prefetcher pre-loads app data on boot. On SSDs, only app launch prefetch is useful.
educationalContext.pros: Reduces unnecessary SSD writes while keeping app launch optimization.
educationalContext.cons: Boot prefetching is disabled (negligible on SSDs).
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Memory Management\PrefetchParameters\EnablePrefetcher' to 1 or 0 (disabling it). Wait, typically for SSDs, Prefetch is disabled or reduced (set to 1 for App launch, 3 is default). Reduces sequential disk caching.
educationalContext.interactions: If 'DisableSysMain' is used, this tweak is technically obsolete since SysMain controls the Prefetcher service engine.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Memory Management\PrefetchParameters' -Name 'EnablePrefetcher' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Memory Management\PrefetchParameters' -Name 'EnableSuperfetch' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force
execution.revertCode: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Memory Management\PrefetchParameters' -Name 'EnablePrefetcher' -Value 3 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Memory Management\PrefetchParameters' -Name 'EnableSuperfetch' -Value 3 -Type DWord -Force

name: Enable Large System Cache
description: Configures Windows to use more RAM for file caching — beneficial for 16GB+ systems.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Tells the memory manager to use more RAM for file system cache instead of limiting it.
educationalContext.pros: Faster file I/O and game loading on systems with plenty of RAM.
educationalContext.cons: May reduce available RAM for applications on 8GB systems. Only for 16GB+.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Memory Management\LargeSystemCache' to 1. Forces Windows to allocate a massive chunk of RAM (up to 1 TB) for the file system cache, reducing disk reads.
educationalContext.interactions: Highly detrimental on systems with < 16GB RAM as it starves active applications of physical memory, forcing them into the pagefile. Excellent for dedicated storage servers.

name: Disable Network Adapter Power Saving
description: Prevents Windows from putting your network adapter to sleep to save power.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Disables 'Allow the computer to turn off this device to save power' for all network adapters.
educationalContext.pros: Eliminates random network disconnects during gaming and high latency spikes.
educationalContext.cons: Slightly higher idle power consumption.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Iterates through network adapters in WMI/CIM and disables 'PnPCapabilities' (Allow the computer to turn off this device). Prevents the network card from sleeping during brief periods of inactivity.
educationalContext.interactions: Fixes connection drops in competitive multiplayer games running on restrictive laptop power plans. Increases power draw.

execution.code: Get-NetAdapter -Physical | Get-NetAdapterPowerManagement -EA SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object { $_.AllowComputerToTurnOffDevice = 'Disabled'; $_ | Set-NetAdapterPowerManagement -EA SilentlyContinue }
execution.revertCode: Get-NetAdapter -Physical | Get-NetAdapterPowerManagement -EA SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object { $_.AllowComputerToTurnOffDevice = 'Enabled'; $_ | Set-NetAdapterPowerManagement -EA SilentlyContinue }

name: Disable Connected Standby
description: Disables Modern Standby (S0ix) and uses classic S3 sleep instead.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Modern Standby keeps the CPU partially active, often causing battery drain and wake issues.
educationalContext.pros: True deep sleep. Eliminates phantom drain and random wake-ups.
educationalContext.cons: Instant-on wake-up is disabled. PC takes 2-3 seconds longer to wake.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power\CsEnabled' to 0 (or 'PlatformAoAcOverride' to 0 in Win11). Disables Modern Standby (S0ix), forcing the computer back to legacy S3 sleep where power is physically cut to the CPU.
educationalContext.interactions: Fixes issues where laptops drain completely in a backpack and overheat because they were downloading Windows Updates while 'asleep'.

name: Maximize MMCSS Gaming Thread Priority
description: Maximizes the Multimedia Class Scheduler priority for gaming threads.

educationalContext.howItWorks: MMCSS manages thread priorities for multimedia/gaming. This maximizes its effect.
educationalContext.pros: Game audio/video threads get maximum scheduling priority.
educationalContext.cons: Non-gaming applications may get slightly less CPU time.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Refines the Multimedia Class Scheduler Service ('HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile'). Adjusts 'NetworkThrottlingIndex' to FFFFFFFF and 'SystemResponsiveness' to 0.
educationalContext.interactions: A superset of 'SystemResponsiveness'. Works alongside 'Optimize TCP/IP Stack' to prevent packet throttling during high-bandwidth media playback or gaming.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile' -Name 'SystemResponsiveness' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile\Tasks\Games' -Name 'Priority' -Value 6 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile\Tasks\Games' -Name 'Scheduling Category' -Value 'High' -Type String -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Multimedia\SystemProfile\Tasks\Games' -Name 'SFIO Priority' -Value 'High' -Type String -Force

name: Disable Xbox Game Monitoring Service
description: Disables the xbgm background monitoring service that tracks game usage.

educationalContext.howItWorks: The xbgm service monitors game sessions for Xbox integration and telemetry.
educationalContext.pros: Removes background monitoring overhead during gaming sessions.
educationalContext.cons: Xbox achievements and play time tracking won't update.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables the 'xbgm' service. This service monitors the execution of games and hooks into them to provide Xbox Live presence and Game Bar metrics.
educationalContext.interactions: Breaks Xbox achievements, Game Pass verification, and Xbox friend invites. Essential to completely detaching Xbox telemetry from Win32 games.

execution.code: schtasks /Change /TN '\Microsoft\XblGameSave\XblGameSaveTask' /Disable 2>$null; schtasks /Change /TN '\Microsoft\XblGameSave\XblGameSaveTaskLogon' /Disable 2>$null; if (Get-Service -Name 'xbgm' -EA SilentlyContinue) { Stop-Service -Name 'xbgm' -Force -EA SilentlyContinue; Set-Service -Name 'xbgm' -StartupType Disabled -EA SilentlyContinue }

name: Reduce Menu Animation Delay
description: Reduces the delay before menus appear from 400ms to 0ms for snappier UI.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets MenuShowDelay registry value to 0, removing the artificial animation pause.
educationalContext.pros: Menus and context menus appear instantly. System feels more responsive.
educationalContext.cons: Purely visual — menus may feel abrupt without the fade-in.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Changes 'MenuShowDelay' in 'HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop' from the default 400ms down to 10ms. Modifies how long you have to hover over a cascaded menu (like 'Send To') before it springs open.
educationalContext.interactions: Makes navigating deeply nested folders or Start Menu folders feel blisteringly fast. Does not impact DWM animations.

name: Optimize Foreground App Priority
description: Sets Win32PrioritySeparation to 0x26 for optimal foreground app scheduling.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Controls how Windows distributes CPU time between foreground and background apps.
educationalContext.pros: Foreground applications get shorter, more responsive quantum time slices.
educationalContext.cons: Background tasks like downloads may be slightly slower.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\PriorityControl\Win32PrioritySeparation' to hex 26 (38 decimal). This adjusts the thread quantum (time slice) allocation, giving shorter, variable time slices prioritizing the foreground application (the active window).
educationalContext.interactions: Highly beneficial for gaming ('GamePriority') and desktop responsiveness, but can slightly reduce the efficiency of heavy background rendering or encoding tasks.

name: Disable Core Isolation / Memory Integrity
description: Disables Virtualization-Based Security memory integrity for 5-10% FPS gain.

educationalContext.howItWorks: VBS/HVCI uses Hyper-V to verify kernel code integrity, adding overhead to every driver call.
educationalContext.pros: Significant FPS improvement (5-10%) in CPU-bound games. Reduces DPC latency.
educationalContext.cons: Disables a key security feature. Not recommended for machines handling sensitive data.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables Hypervisor-protected Code Integrity (HVCI) via 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\DeviceGuard\Scenarios\HypervisorEnforcedCodeIntegrity'. This feature uses virtualization to protect the kernel, but introduces severe virtualization overhead during gaming cache misses.
educationalContext.interactions: Disabling it can improve CPU-bound 1% lows by 5-15%, but significantly reduces security against sophisticated kernel-level rootkits (and some anti-cheat systems).

name: Disable Aero Shake
description: Prevents shaking a window from minimizing all other windows.

educationalContext.howItWorks: When you shake a title bar, Windows minimizes everything else. This disables that behavior.
educationalContext.pros: No more accidental minimization when dragging windows.
educationalContext.cons: You lose the shake-to-minimize gesture.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables 'DisallowShaking' in 'HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer'. Prevents Windows from automatically minimizing all other windows when you grab a title bar and 'shake' the mouse.
educationalContext.interactions: Prevents accidental mass-minimization during frantic mouse movements in windowed games or intense workflows.

name: Disable Lock Screen
description: Skips the lock screen and goes straight to the password/PIN prompt.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets NoLockScreen policy so Windows goes directly to the login prompt.
educationalContext.pros: Saves a click/swipe every time you unlock. Faster access.
educationalContext.cons: No at-a-glance info (time, notifications) on the lock screen.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'NoLockScreen' (DWORD 1) in 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization'. Entirely skips the graphical lock screen (the one showing the clock and wallpaper).
educationalContext.interactions: Takes you instantly from wake/boot to the password/PIN entry screen. Saves exactly one click/keypress every single time you wake the PC.

name: Clear Page File at Shutdown
description: Clears the virtual memory page file when Windows shuts down for security.

educationalContext.howItWorks: The page file can contain sensitive data swapped from RAM. This zeros it out at shutdown.
educationalContext.pros: Prevents extracting passwords/keys from the page file on stolen drives.
educationalContext.cons: Shutdown takes slightly longer (depends on page file size).
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'ClearPageFileAtShutdown' to 1 in the Session Manager\Memory Management. Forces Windows to securely overwrite the pagefile.sys with zeros on shutdown.
educationalContext.interactions: Increases shutdown times drastically (can take minutes on a slow HDD), but ensures extracted passwords or encryption keys aren't left behind in the swap on cold rest.

name: Enable Last Active Click (Taskbar)
description: Clicking a grouped taskbar icon opens the last used window instead of showing a preview.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets LastActiveClick to 1 so taskbar clicks go directly to the last active window.
educationalContext.pros: One click instead of two to switch to your last used instance of an app.
educationalContext.cons: You must hover (not click) to see the preview thumbnail.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'LastActiveClick' in 'Explorer\Advanced' to 1. If you have multiple windows of the same app open (e.g., 3 Chrome windows), clicking the taskbar icon will immediately open the last active one instead of forcing you to choose from the thumbnail preview popup.
educationalContext.interactions: Massively improves taskbar multitasking ergonomics for power users.

name: Repair System Files (SFC + DISM)
description: Runs DISM and SFC to repair corrupted Windows system files.

educationalContext.howItWorks: DISM repairs the component store, then SFC scans and fixes protected system files.
educationalContext.pros: Fixes random crashes, blue screens, and broken features from corrupted files.
educationalContext.cons: Takes 5-15 minutes. Requires internet for DISM to download clean files.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Executes 'DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth' followed cleanly by 'sfc /scannow'. Connects to Windows Update to download uncorrupted versions of core system files and violently overwrites damaged local caches.
educationalContext.interactions: Takes 5-20 minutes depending on SSD speed. Best run whenever experiencing random application crashes or blue screens.

execution.revertCode: Write-Output 'System file repair cannot be reversed (it restores original files).'

name: Disable Search Highlights & Web Results
description: Removes Bing web results and trending highlights from Windows Search.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Disables dynamic content and web integration in the Start Menu search.
educationalContext.pros: Faster, cleaner search that only shows local files and apps.
educationalContext.cons: You won't see web suggestions or trending topics in search.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables the daily rotating images and 'fun facts' (Search Highlights) inside the Start Menu search interface by disabling the 'DynamicSearchBoxEnabled' DWORD.
educationalContext.interactions: Makes the Start menu search UI completely blank and sterile, exactly like older robust versions of Windows.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\SearchSettings' -Name 'IsDynamicSearchBoxEnabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; if (!(Test-Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer')) { New-Item -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer' -Name 'DisableSearchBoxSuggestions' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force

name: Disable Windows Recall AI
description: Disables Windows Recall that continuously screenshots your activity.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows Recall takes periodic screenshots and indexes them with AI. This disables it.
educationalContext.pros: Prevents AI from recording everything you do on your PC.
educationalContext.cons: You lose the ability to search past activity with natural language.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Stops Windows 11's 'Recall' feature by terminating its associated background scanning service and disabling the group policy. Recall takes constant screenshots of the desktop to create an AI-searchable timeline.
educationalContext.interactions: Massive privacy and security gain. Prevents gigabytes of local SSD space from being consumed by unencrypted OCR screenshots.

execution.code: if (!(Test-Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsAI')) { New-Item -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsAI' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsAI' -Name 'DisableAIDataAnalysis' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force; if (!(Test-Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsAI')) { New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsAI' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsAI' -Name 'DisableAIDataAnalysis' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force

name: Disable Phone Link / Your Phone
description: Removes the Phone Link app integration and background service.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Uninstalls the Your Phone / Phone Link app and blocks its background service.
educationalContext.pros: Removes background syncing with your phone that uses CPU and network.
educationalContext.cons: You can't mirror Android notifications or texts to your PC.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Uninstalls the 'Microsoft.YourPhone' AppX package. This app constantly runs a background suspended process to listen for Bluetooth beacons from paired phones.
educationalContext.interactions: Completely breaks the ability to mirror Android notifications, SMS, or calls to your PC.

execution.code: Get-AppxPackage -Name 'Microsoft.YourPhone' -AllUsers -EA SilentlyContinue | Remove-AppxPackage -AllUsers -EA SilentlyContinue; if (!(Test-Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System')) { New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System' -Name 'EnableMmx' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force
execution.revertCode: Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System' -Name 'EnableMmx' -EA SilentlyContinue; Write-Output 'Reinstall Phone Link from the Microsoft Store.'

name: Disable Notification Center & Tips
description: Disables Windows notification popups and tip balloons.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Disables toast notifications and tip notifications system-wide.
educationalContext.pros: No more popup distractions during gaming or focused work.
educationalContext.cons: You won't see any app notifications until you re-enable.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Enables 'DisableNotificationCenter' under Explorer policies. Completely nukes the Action Center sidebar and stops all toast notifications from rendering.
educationalContext.interactions: A draconian UI cleanup. You will miss USB plugin sounds, email toasts, and system warnings. Use with caution.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PushNotifications' -Name 'ToastEnabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Notifications\Settings' -Name 'NOC_GLOBAL_SETTING_TOASTS_ENABLED' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force -EA SilentlyContinue; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer' -Name 'DisableNotificationCenter' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force -EA SilentlyContinue

name: Enable GPU MSI Mode (Message Signaled Interrupts)
description: Enables MSI mode for your GPU, reducing interrupt latency for smoother gaming.

educationalContext.howItWorks: MSI replaces line-based interrupts with message-based ones, reducing DPC latency.
educationalContext.pros: Lower DPC latency (1-3ms reduction). Smoother frame delivery. Reduces micro-stutters.
educationalContext.cons: May cause BSODs on unsupported hardware. Research your GPU model first.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies individual PCI registry keys under 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Enum\PCI' to enable Message Signaled Interrupts (MSI) for the GPU. Replaces legacy Line-Based IRQ sharing, allowing the GPU to send interrupt signals without polling wait times.
educationalContext.interactions: Solves DPC Latency spikes (dxgkrnl.sys). If a GPU driver doesn't support MSI natively, forcing this will cause a BSOD loop on boot.

execution.code: $gpuDevice = Get-PnpDevice -Class Display -Status OK | Select-Object -First 1; if ($gpuDevice) { $instanceId = $gpuDevice.InstanceId -replace '\\','\\'; $path = "HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Enum\$($gpuDevice.InstanceId)\Device Parameters\Interrupt Management\MessageSignaledInterruptProperties"; if (!(Test-Path $path)) { New-Item -Path $path -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path $path -Name 'MSISupported' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force }
execution.revertCode: $gpuDevice = Get-PnpDevice -Class Display -Status OK | Select-Object -First 1; if ($gpuDevice) { $path = "HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Enum\$($gpuDevice.InstanceId)\Device Parameters\Interrupt Management\MessageSignaledInterruptProperties"; Set-ItemProperty -Path $path -Name 'MSISupported' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force -EA SilentlyContinue }

name: Disable Edge Pre-launch & Pre-load
description: Prevents Microsoft Edge from pre-launching and pre-loading in the background.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Edge starts background processes at boot even if you use another browser.
educationalContext.pros: Saves 100-200MB RAM from Edge processes running in the background.
educationalContext.cons: Edge will take 1-2 seconds longer to launch when you do use it.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'TabPreloader' and 'Prelaunch' keys in Edge policies to prevent Microsoft Edge from silently launching invisible processes on boot to make the browser launch 'faster' when clicked.
educationalContext.interactions: Saves around 40-60MB of RAM on boot. Edge will take ~0.5s longer to open visually the first time.

execution.code: if (!(Test-Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\MicrosoftEdge\Main')) { New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\MicrosoftEdge\Main' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\MicrosoftEdge\Main' -Name 'AllowPrelaunch' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; if (!(Test-Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\MicrosoftEdge\TabPreloader')) { New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\MicrosoftEdge\TabPreloader' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\MicrosoftEdge\TabPreloader' -Name 'AllowTabPreloading' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force

name: Expand Ephemeral Port Range
description: Expands the dynamic TCP/UDP port range to 1025–65535, reducing port exhaustion under heavy connection loads (servers, gaming, streaming).

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows defaults to ports 49152–65535 for outbound connections (~16K ports). Expanding to 64K+ ports prevents 'no ports available' failures under high concurrency.
educationalContext.pros: Prevents connection failures in high-traffic scenarios. Useful for game servers, streaming software, and heavily multi-threaded downloaders.
educationalContext.cons: Negligible. Wider range just means more ports available; doesn't open inbound attack surface.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Uses 'netsh int ipv4 set dynamicport tcp start=1025 num=64511'. Ephemeral ports are short-lived transport protocol ports. Expanding the default pool (which is often restricted to 16,384 ports) prevents port exhaustion during heavy torrenting, server loads, or extreme multitasking.
educationalContext.interactions: Only necessary if you regularly open thousands of concurrent connections. Safe baseline tweak.

execution.code: netsh int ipv4 set dynamicport tcp start=1025 num=64511; netsh int ipv4 set dynamicport udp start=1025 num=64511
execution.revertCode: netsh int ipv4 set dynamicport tcp start=49152 num=16384; netsh int ipv4 set dynamicport udp start=49152 num=16384

validationCmd: (netsh int ipv4 show dynamicport tcp) | Select-String 'Start Port'

name: Reduce TCP TIME_WAIT Delay
description: Reduces the time TCP connections linger in the TIME_WAIT state from 4 minutes to 30 seconds, allowing ports to be recycled faster.

educationalContext.howItWorks: After a TCP connection closes, Windows holds the port in TIME_WAIT for 240 seconds (default) to catch late-arriving packets. Most modern protocols don't need this long.
educationalContext.pros: Faster port recycling improves throughput for apps that open many short-lived connections (REST APIs, game matchmaking, download managers).
educationalContext.cons: Very rare: if packets arrive more than 30s after connection close, they could be mis-associated with a new connection on the same port.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'TcpTimedWaitDelay' in 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters' to 30. Changes how long a closed TCP connection stays in the TIME_WAIT state before the socket is released back to the OS pool (from default 120s to 30s).
educationalContext.interactions: Prevents socket exhaustion if you rapidly connect/disconnect from dozens of IPs. Works perfectly alongside 'ExpandEphemeralPorts'.

name: Optimize DNS Cache Size
description: Increases the Windows DNS cache from its conservative default to 4096 entries and eliminates negative TTL caching, speeding up domain lookups.

educationalContext.howItWorks: The DNS Client service caches resolved hostnames locally. A larger cache means fewer round-trips to DNS servers. Zeroing MaxNegativeCacheTtl prevents caching of failed lookups that may later succeed.
educationalContext.pros: Faster page loads and connection establishment for frequently visited domains. Reduces DNS server load.
educationalContext.cons: Slightly more RAM used for the cache (negligible, < 1 MB). Stale entries could persist longer if a DNS record changes mid-session.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Increases 'MaxCacheTtl' (to 86400) and 'MaxNegativeCacheTtl' (to 0) in 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Dnscache\Parameters'. Caches successful DNS queries for 24 hours and ignores negative (failed) queries entirely.
educationalContext.interactions: Speeds up initial connection to heavily visited websites by skipping the 15-30ms DNS resolution phase. Disabling negative cache prevents 'stuck' unresolvable domains.

name: Enable Receive-Side Scaling (RSS)
description: Ensures RSS is enabled so incoming network traffic is distributed across multiple CPU cores, preventing a single core from becoming a bottleneck.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Without RSS, all network interrupts are handled by CPU 0 alone. RSS spreads the interrupt processing across cores, improving throughput on multi-core systems under sustained network load.
educationalContext.pros: Higher sustained throughput, lower CPU usage per-core, reduced network latency under heavy loads.
educationalContext.cons: None on modern hardware. RSS is a standard feature supported by all modern NICs.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Uses 'netsh int tcp set global rss=enabled'. Receive-Side Scaling (RSS) allows the network adapter hardware to distribute inbound network processing across multiple CPU cores rather than slamming Core 0.
educationalContext.interactions: Relies entirely on your network adapter supporting RSS (most do). If disabled, high-bandwidth transfers will bottleneck on a single CPU thread.

execution.code: netsh int tcp set global rss=enabled
execution.revertCode: netsh int tcp set global rss=disabled

validationCmd: (netsh int tcp show global) | Select-String 'Receive-Side Scaling State'

name: Disable TCP Auto-Tuning
description: Disables Windows' automatic TCP receive-window scaling, which can cause inconsistent throughput on connections with high latency or packet loss.

educationalContext.howItWorks: TCP Auto-Tuning dynamically adjusts the receive buffer window size. On some networks (high-latency satellite, VPN, enterprise firewalls) this can cause throughput to oscillate unpredictably.
educationalContext.pros: More stable, predictable throughput on problematic connections. Can improve gaming latency on certain ISP configurations.
educationalContext.cons: On a healthy broadband connection, auto-tuning typically achieves better peak throughput than a fixed window. Only disable if experiencing instability.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'netsh int tcp set global autotuninglevel=disabled'. Restricts the TCP Receive Window to a flat 64KB instead of allowing it to scale dynamically up to 16MB.
educationalContext.interactions: Warning: Only use this if you are on an archaic connection (e.g. < 5 Mbps) or specific VPNs that misbehave. On modern fiber/cable connections, disabling auto-tuning crushes download speeds to 10-20% of their maximum.

execution.code: netsh int tcp set global autotuninglevel=disabled
execution.revertCode: netsh int tcp set global autotuninglevel=normal

validationCmd: (netsh int tcp show global) | Select-String 'Receive Window Auto-Tuning Level'

name: Disable NetBIOS over TCP/IP
description: Disables the legacy NetBIOS protocol on all active network adapters. Not needed in modern pure-IP environments and eliminates associated attack surface.

educationalContext.howItWorks: NetBIOS is a 1980s protocol used for Windows network discovery and name resolution in pre-Active Directory environments. It generates background broadcast traffic and has known security vulnerabilities (LLMNR poisoning, NBNS spoofing).
educationalContext.pros: Eliminates broadcast pollution on the network. Removes NBNS/LLMNR attack vectors. Reduces network interface CPU overhead.
educationalContext.cons: Breaks network discovery on legacy Windows workgroups (Windows XP-era file sharing). Do not disable on corporate networks relying on old NetBIOS-dependent software.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables NetBIOS over TCP/IP in the WMI network adapter configuration. NetBIOS is a legacy broadcast protocol used for older networked file sharing.
educationalContext.interactions: Reduces network broadcast noise and closes an archaic local attack vector. Will break access to old NAS devices or Windows XP/7 shared folders.

name: Disable Fast Startup
description: Disables Windows Fast Startup (hybrid boot). Ensures full cold boots that apply all pending driver and Windows updates correctly on restart.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Fast Startup saves a partial hibernation file on shutdown so the next boot resumes from that state rather than doing a full cold boot. This means drivers and system state are not fully re-initialized.
educationalContext.pros: Fixes driver-loading issues, ensures Windows Updates apply on restart, prevents stale system state that can cause BSoDs or performance regressions after updates.
educationalContext.cons: Slightly slower cold boot (typically 3–8 seconds longer). The difference is negligible on NVMe SSDs.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Power\HiberbootEnabled' to 0. Fast Startup uses a hybrid logoff/hibernate sequence to boot faster. Disabling it ensures a 'True' shutdown, clearing full kernel memory and driver states.
educationalContext.interactions: Increases cold boot time by ~2-5 seconds on SSDs, but resolves 99% of 'uptime'-related memory leaks and driver desync issues.

name: Disable USB Selective Suspend
description: Prevents Windows from powering down idle USB devices. Eliminates the reconnection stutter experienced with USB headsets, controllers, and audio interfaces.

educationalContext.howItWorks: USB Selective Suspend lets Windows cut power to idle USB devices to save energy. When the device is needed again, it must wake up — causing a 50–500ms dropout that can disrupt audio, input, or storage.
educationalContext.pros: No more USB audio dropouts or controller disconnects during gameplay. Zero-latency USB device responses.
educationalContext.cons: Higher idle power consumption (typically 1–3W for all USB devices combined). Not recommended if maximizing laptop battery life is a priority.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'powercfg' specific index keys for the USB profile. Disables the mechanism where the USB hub driver selectively cuts power to individual USB ports when devices are 'idle'.
educationalContext.interactions: Fixes USB audio DAC popping, mouse sensor polling rate drops, and VR headset disconnections caused by aggressive power gating.

execution.code: powercfg -setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT 2a737441-1930-4402-8d77-b2bebba308a3 48e6b7a6-50f5-4782-a5d4-53bb8f07e226 0; powercfg -setdcvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT 2a737441-1930-4402-8d77-b2bebba308a3 48e6b7a6-50f5-4782-a5d4-53bb8f07e226 0; powercfg -setactive SCHEME_CURRENT
execution.revertCode: powercfg -setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT 2a737441-1930-4402-8d77-b2bebba308a3 48e6b7a6-50f5-4782-a5d4-53bb8f07e226 1; powercfg -setdcvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT 2a737441-1930-4402-8d77-b2bebba308a3 48e6b7a6-50f5-4782-a5d4-53bb8f07e226 1; powercfg -setactive SCHEME_CURRENT

name: Disable PCIe Link State Power Management
description: Prevents the PCIe bus from entering low-power states, ensuring GPU and NVMe SSD remain at full performance without latency spikes from power state transitions.

educationalContext.howItWorks: PCIe Active State Power Management (ASPM) allows devices to enter L1/L0s low-power states when idle. Transitioning out of these states takes 10–40µs which causes micro-stutters in games and storage workloads.
educationalContext.pros: Eliminates GPU micro-stutters and NVMe latency spikes caused by PCIe power state transitions. Particularly noticeable in 1% low frametimes in benchmarks.
educationalContext.cons: Slightly higher system idle power draw (typically 2–5W). No impact on laptop battery life is minimal if you're gaming on AC anyway.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies Active State Power Management (ASPM) for the PCI Express bus via 'powercfg'. Changes the link state from 'Maximum power savings' to 'Off'.
educationalContext.interactions: Crucial for preventing NVMe SSDs and discrete GPUs from dropping PCIe gen bandwidth during low loads, which strictly reduces latency spikes.

execution.code: powercfg -setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_PCIEXPRESS ASPM 0; powercfg -setactive SCHEME_CURRENT
execution.revertCode: powercfg -setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_PCIEXPRESS ASPM 2; powercfg -setactive SCHEME_CURRENT

name: Enable Aggressive CPU Boost Mode
description: Sets CPU performance boost to Aggressive mode, allowing the processor to reach and sustain its maximum boost clock more readily for compute-intensive workloads.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows exposes a CPU performance boost setting through the power plan. Aggressive mode instructs the processor to boost to its turbo frequency more eagerly and maintain it longer, versus Efficient Aggressive which is the common default.
educationalContext.pros: Higher sustained CPU boost clocks. Reduced latency in single-threaded workloads. Measurable improvement in lightly-threaded games.
educationalContext.cons: Slightly higher CPU temperature and power draw under sustained boost. On systems without adequate cooling, may cause more thermal throttling.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies powercfg PROCFREQ attributes (PerfBoostMode) to 'Aggressive'. Forces the CPU governor to respond instantly to any workload spike by raising P-state voltage/clocks.
educationalContext.interactions: Mainly applicable to Ryzen CPUs (CPPC2). Can cause fan RPM spikes ('yo-yo' acoustics) as temperature fluctuates violently with minimal loads.

execution.code: powercfg -setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_PROCESSOR PERFBOOSTMODE 2; powercfg -setactive SCHEME_CURRENT
execution.revertCode: powercfg -setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_PROCESSOR PERFBOOSTMODE 1; powercfg -setactive SCHEME_CURRENT

name: Disable Adaptive Display Brightness
description: Stops Windows from automatically adjusting display brightness based on ambient light. Provides consistent, predictable brightness levels.

educationalContext.howItWorks: On systems with an ambient light sensor, Windows can dim or brighten the display automatically. This often triggers unexpectedly during gameplay, video watching, or screen recording.
educationalContext.pros: Consistent brightness without unexpected dimming. Eliminates brightness drift during gaming sessions or screen recordings.
educationalContext.cons: You must manually adjust brightness for ambient lighting changes. Slightly higher display power draw in bright environments.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies display power configuration settings. Disables Intel DPST / AMD Vari-Bright via registry or powercfg commands, which dynamically lowers screen brightness based on the dark/light content of the currently rendered frames.
educationalContext.interactions: Restores accurate color rendering and prevents annoying brightness flickering, at the cost of slight battery drain.

execution.code: powercfg -setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_VIDEO ADAPTBRIGHT 0; powercfg -setdcvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_VIDEO ADAPTBRIGHT 0; powercfg -setactive SCHEME_CURRENT
execution.revertCode: powercfg -setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_VIDEO ADAPTBRIGHT 1; powercfg -setdcvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_VIDEO ADAPTBRIGHT 1; powercfg -setactive SCHEME_CURRENT

name: Lock CPU Minimum Performance to 100%
description: Forces the CPU to always operate at 100% of its performance state, eliminating clock speed ramp-up delays at the cost of higher idle power draw.

educationalContext.howItWorks: By default, Windows allows the CPU to idle at as low as 5% of its performance state (e.g., 400 MHz on a 4 GHz chip). Setting the minimum to 100% prevents downclocking, so the CPU is always ready to respond instantly.
educationalContext.pros: Zero latency to reach full CPU frequency. Eliminates 'wake-up' stutters at the start of frames in CPU-bound games. Measurable improvement in minimum frametimes.
educationalContext.cons: Significantly higher idle power and heat. CPU will not throttle down when idle, increasing electricity use by 10–30W. Not recommended for laptops or thermally constrained systems.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Executes 'powercfg -setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_PROCESSOR PROCTHROTTLEMIN 100'. Forces the CPU to permanently run at its base/boost clock, never downclocking to idle frequencies (e.g. 800MHz).
educationalContext.interactions: Causes extremely high idle temperatures. Provides zero discernible performance gain over the standard 'High Performance' plan which already ramps effectively.

execution.code: powercfg -setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_PROCESSOR PROCTHROTTLEMIN 100; powercfg -setactive SCHEME_CURRENT
execution.revertCode: powercfg -setacvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT SUB_PROCESSOR PROCTHROTTLEMIN 5; powercfg -setactive SCHEME_CURRENT

name: Enable Ultimate Performance Power Plan
description: Unhides and activates the Ultimate Performance power plan — Microsoft's highest performance preset.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows ships with a hidden 'Ultimate Performance' plan that disables all power-saving features. This command enables it.
educationalContext.pros: Maximum CPU/GPU performance. Eliminates all power-saving latency. Best for desktop gaming rigs.
educationalContext.cons: Higher power consumption and heat. Not suitable for laptops on battery.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Duplicate of UltimatePowerPlan (e9a42b02-d5df-448d-aa00-03f14749eb61). Enables Microsoft's Enterprise power profile.
educationalContext.interactions: Overwrites existing Active power plan. Highly recommended to use this instead of manually hacking individual Powercfg PROCFREQ flags.

name: Increase IRPStackSize
description: Increases the I/O Request Packet stack size for better network file sharing performance.

educationalContext.howItWorks: IRPStackSize controls how many I/O request packets the network stack can queue. Default of 15 is too low for many NAS/SMB setups.
educationalContext.pros: Faster SMB file transfers. Eliminates 'insufficient server storage' errors.
educationalContext.cons: Negligible additional memory usage. Safe on all systems.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'IRPStackSize' in 'HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanServer\Parameters'. Allocates more memory stack for the local Server service to handle concurrent network file I/O commands.
educationalContext.interactions: Fixes 'Not enough server storage is available' errors when copying massive files over gigabit/10Gb LAN.

name: Enable DNS over HTTPS (DoH)
description: Enables DNS over HTTPS to encrypt DNS queries and prevent ISP snooping.

educationalContext.howItWorks: DoH wraps DNS queries in HTTPS, preventing ISPs and network observers from seeing what domains you visit.
educationalContext.pros: Encrypted DNS queries. ISP can't see your browsing domains. Prevents DNS hijacking.
educationalContext.cons: Requires Windows 11 or higher. Slightly higher latency on first lookup.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Configures Native Windows 11 DoH strictly via 'netsh dns add global auto'. Encrypts your DNS requests using HTTPS so your ISP cannot read or tamper with them.
educationalContext.interactions: Provides immense privacy. You MUST use a supported DNS provider (like Cloudflare, Quad9, Google) as your primary DNS for this to take effect.

name: Speed Up Shutdown Time
description: Reduces WaitToKillServiceTimeout and HungAppTimeout for faster shutdowns.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows waits up to 5 seconds for services and hung apps to close. This reduces it to 2 seconds.
educationalContext.pros: Shutdown and restart complete noticeably faster.
educationalContext.cons: Apps with unsaved data may not get enough time to save. Most apps auto-save.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'WaitToKillServiceTimeout' in 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control' to heavily reduce the time (from 5000ms to 2000ms) Windows waits for services to close gracefully before force-killing them during shutdown.
educationalContext.interactions: Can cause data loss if background applications (like database servers or disk write caches) are forcibly terminated before they finish saving state.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control' -Name 'WaitToKillServiceTimeout' -Value '2000' -Type String -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\Control Panel\Desktop' -Name 'HungAppTimeout' -Value '2000' -Type String -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\Control Panel\Desktop' -Name 'WaitToKillAppTimeout' -Value '2000' -Type String -Force
execution.revertCode: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control' -Name 'WaitToKillServiceTimeout' -Value '5000' -Type String -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\Control Panel\Desktop' -Name 'HungAppTimeout' -Value '5000' -Type String -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\Control Panel\Desktop' -Name 'WaitToKillAppTimeout' -Value '20000' -Type String -Force

name: Disable Advertising ID
description: Disables your unique advertising identifier that Microsoft uses to track you across apps.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows assigns a unique advertising ID to your profile for targeted ads in apps and browsers.
educationalContext.pros: Apps can no longer track you via Microsoft's advertising ID.
educationalContext.cons: You may see less relevant ads in free Microsoft Store apps.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'Enabled' to 0 in 'HKCU\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AdvertisingInfo'. Disables the unique device GUID that Microsoft apps use to provide personalized retargeted advertisements across the OS.
educationalContext.interactions: Ads in Windows apps will still appear, but they will be generic rather than tracked to your personal Microsoft Account behavior.

name: Disable Location Capability Access
description: Disables Windows location services and prevents apps from accessing your location.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows collects location data via GPS, Wi-Fi, and IP geolocation. This disables it system-wide.
educationalContext.pros: No app or Microsoft service can access your physical location.
educationalContext.cons: Weather, Maps, and Find My Device won't work correctly.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Similar to DisableLocationTracking but explicitly targets App Privacy capability overrides.
educationalContext.interactions: Redundant if the location service is fully disabled.

name: Disable Clipboard Cloud History
description: Prevents clipboard contents from being synced to the cloud across devices.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows syncs clipboard data (including copied passwords) across your Microsoft account devices.
educationalContext.pros: Your clipboard contents (passwords, sensitive text) stay local.
educationalContext.cons: You can't paste between your Windows devices via the cloud.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Duplicate or alias for 'DisableClipboardSync'.
educationalContext.interactions: Redundant if clipboard sync is already stopped.

name: Flush DNS Cache
description: Clears the local DNS resolver cache to fix stale DNS entries and resolve connectivity issues.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Clears all cached DNS lookups. The next connection will do a fresh DNS resolution.
educationalContext.pros: Fixes websites not loading after DNS changes. Resolves stale cache entries.
educationalContext.cons: First visit to each site will be slightly slower (one DNS lookup). Cache rebuilds in seconds.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Duplicate alias for FlushDNS.
educationalContext.interactions: Same as FlushDNS.

execution.revertCode: Write-Output 'DNS cache is rebuilt automatically as you browse.'

name: Reset Winsock Catalog
description: Resets the Winsock catalog to fix network connectivity issues caused by corrupted LSP entries.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Winsock is Windows' network socket API. Corrupted entries from VPNs or security software can break all networking.
educationalContext.pros: Fixes 'no internet' issues, DNS failures, and corrupted network stacks.
educationalContext.cons: Requires a reboot. Some VPN/proxy software may need reinstalling.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Duplicate alias handling network resets.
educationalContext.interactions: Requires a reboot.

execution.code: netsh winsock reset; netsh int ip reset
execution.revertCode: Write-Output 'Winsock reset cannot be explicitly reversed. Network will reconfigure on reboot.'

name: Block Cortana
description: Disables Cortana and prevents it from running in the background.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Cortana runs as a background process even when not in use, consuming RAM and sending data to Microsoft.
educationalContext.pros: Saves 50-100MB RAM. No more background data collection by Cortana.
educationalContext.cons: Voice search and Cortana features will not work.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Duplicate or alias for 'DisableCortana'.
educationalContext.interactions: Ensures Cortana is fully disabled across different feature updates.

execution.code: if (!(Test-Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search')) { New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search' -Name 'AllowCortana' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search' -Name 'AllowCortanaAboveLock' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force
execution.revertCode: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search' -Name 'AllowCortana' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force; Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search' -Name 'AllowCortanaAboveLock' -EA SilentlyContinue

name: Enable 'End Task' in Taskbar Right-Click
description: Adds 'End Task' to the taskbar right-click context menu (Win11 23H2+).

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows 11 23H2+ has a hidden 'End Task' option in the taskbar right-click menu. This enables it.
educationalContext.pros: Kill frozen apps directly from the taskbar without opening Task Manager.
educationalContext.cons: None. This is a hidden QoL feature that Microsoft should have enabled by default.
educationalContext.expertDetails: A Windows 11 23H2+ exclusive. Sets 'TaskbarEndTask' to 1 in DeveloperSettings. Adds a native 'End Task' button when right-clicking any app icon on the taskbar.
educationalContext.interactions: Allows instantly force-killing a hung application (SIGKILL) without needing to ever open Task Manager.

name: Disable Sticky Keys Popup
description: Disables the Sticky Keys popup when pressing Shift 5 times — essential for gaming.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Pressing Shift 5 times triggers a Sticky Keys accessibility prompt. This disables that shortcut.
educationalContext.pros: No more annoying popups during gaming when spamming Shift.
educationalContext.cons: You lose the Shift×5 shortcut to enable Sticky Keys (still accessible via Settings).
educationalContext.expertDetails: Overrides 'Flags' under 'HKCU\Control Panel\Accessibility\StickyKeys'. Turns off the atrocious shortcut that interrupts games when mashing the SHIFT key 5 times.
educationalContext.interactions: Critical for gaming. Has zero negative impact unless you legitimately require single-finger modifier key sequences.

name: Disable IPv6
description: Disables IPv6 on all network adapters. Can fix connectivity issues on IPv4-only networks.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Disables the IPv6 protocol stack. Most consumer ISPs still primarily use IPv4.
educationalContext.pros: Fixes DNS leaks in some VPN configs. Can resolve connectivity issues on IPv4-only networks.
educationalContext.cons: Breaks IPv6-only services. Some modern apps and services are moving to IPv6.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Unbinds the IPv6 protocol component from all active network adapters using 'Disable-NetAdapterBinding'.
educationalContext.interactions: Breaks Xbox Live Teredo tunneling, HomeGroup (legacy), and some modern ISPs that use native IPv6 routing. Only applies if your router or VPN struggles with dual-stack leakage.

execution.code: Get-NetAdapterBinding -ComponentID ms_tcpip6 -EA SilentlyContinue | Disable-NetAdapterBinding -ComponentID ms_tcpip6 -EA SilentlyContinue; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip6\Parameters' -Name 'DisabledComponents' -Value 255 -Type DWord -Force
execution.revertCode: Get-NetAdapterBinding -ComponentID ms_tcpip6 -EA SilentlyContinue | Enable-NetAdapterBinding -ComponentID ms_tcpip6 -EA SilentlyContinue; Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip6\Parameters' -Name 'DisabledComponents' -EA SilentlyContinue

name: Disable Windows Platform Binary Table
description: Blocks OEMs from force-installing software via UEFI firmware on every boot.

educationalContext.howItWorks: WPBT lets motherboard vendors embed executables in UEFI that Windows runs automatically at boot.
educationalContext.pros: Prevents vendor bloatware from re-installing itself. Closes a firmware-level attack vector.
educationalContext.cons: Some vendor-specific features (anti-theft, driver helpers) may stop working.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'DisableWpbtExecution' to 1 in 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager'. Windows Platform Binary Table allows OEM BIOS firmware (like Lenovo/Dell) to auto-inject bloatware into the OS on every boot, even on a clean install.
educationalContext.interactions: A brilliant defense against persistent OEM bloatware like 'Lenovo Vantage' silently resurrecting itself.

name: Disable Multiplane Overlay (MPO)
description: Disables MPO which can cause black screens, flickering, and stuttering on some GPU/monitor combos.

educationalContext.howItWorks: MPO lets the GPU composite multiple planes (overlays) in hardware. Buggy implementations cause visual glitches.
educationalContext.pros: Fixes black screens, flickering, and stuttering in games on affected GPUs (esp. AMD pre-RDNA3).
educationalContext.cons: Slightly higher GPU power usage for desktop compositing. No impact on modern NVIDIA cards.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Adds 'OverlayTestMode' DWORD (Value 5) to 'HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\Dwm'. Disables MPO, a feature that allows the GPU composer to bypass the DWM for windowed video/gaming.
educationalContext.interactions: Fixes severe black screen flickering, alt-tab stuttering, and cursor trails on specific NVIDIA RTX / AMD RX GPU drivers.

name: Remove Home from File Explorer
description: Removes the 'Home' page from File Explorer in Windows 11 and opens to 'This PC' instead.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets Explorer to open to This PC instead of the Home view that shows recent files and favorites.
educationalContext.pros: Faster access to drives. No privacy-leaking recent files shown on Explorer open.
educationalContext.cons: You lose the quick access to recent files and favorites on the Home page.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Changes 'LaunchTo' to 1 (This PC) and removes the 'HomeFolder' Quick Access items. Booting File Explorer opens directly to your drives instead of a messy list of recent generic files.
educationalContext.interactions: Drastically cleans up the navigation pane. Restores legacy Windows 7/10 'My Computer' behavior.

name: Disable Start Menu Recommendations & Suggestions
description: Removes app suggestions and recommendations from the Windows 11 Start Menu.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows 11 shows 'Recommended' apps and tips in the Start Menu. This disables them.
educationalContext.pros: Cleaner Start Menu with no ads or suggestions. Faster Start Menu rendering.
educationalContext.cons: You won't see recently opened files or app suggestions in Start.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables 'ShowRecommendations' in the Windows 11 Start Menu, pushing the 'Recommended' section down and trying to force it to show solely pinned apps.
educationalContext.interactions: On Windows 11, this leaves a large blank empty space in the Start Menu unless you also use a third-party tool like StartAllBack to modify the UI.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced' -Name 'Start_TrackProgs' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; if (!(Test-Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer')) { New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer' -Name 'HideRecommendedSection' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force

name: Set Time to UTC (Dual Boot Fix)
description: Makes Windows use UTC for the hardware clock — essential for dual-booting with Linux.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Linux uses UTC for the hardware clock, Windows uses local time. This makes both consistent.
educationalContext.pros: Fixes the time being wrong every time you switch between Linux and Windows.
educationalContext.cons: None if you're dual-booting. Slight confusion if you later remove Linux.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets 'RealTimeIsUniversal' (DWORD 1) in 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\TimeZoneInformation'. Forces Windows to treat the motherboard hardware clock (RTC) as pure UTC instead of local time.
educationalContext.interactions: The absolute cure-all for time synchronization bugs when dual-booting Windows and Linux (Ubuntu/Arch).

name: Disable App Launch Tracking
description: Stops Windows from tracking which apps you launch and how often.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows records every app launch to personalize Start Menu and search results. This stops that tracking.
educationalContext.pros: No more app launch history recorded by Windows.
educationalContext.cons: Start Menu 'Most used' apps section won't update.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Stops Windows from tracking which Win32 applications you launch. Normally tracked to populate the 'Most Used' list in the Start Menu.
educationalContext.interactions: The 'Most Used' section in the Start Menu will be blank or freeze updating.

name: Disable Feedback Requests
description: Stops Windows from asking for feedback and sending diagnostic feedback data.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows periodically prompts you for feedback about your experience. This silences it.
educationalContext.pros: No more 'How likely are you to recommend Windows?' popups.
educationalContext.cons: Microsoft won't receive your feedback about bugs or features.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Duplicate or alias for 'DisableFeedback'. Sets frequency to 'Never'.
educationalContext.interactions: Quietens the OS.

execution.code: if (!(Test-Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Siuf\Rules')) { New-Item -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Siuf\Rules' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Siuf\Rules' -Name 'NumberOfSIUFInPeriod' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Siuf\Rules' -Name 'PeriodInNanoSeconds' -EA SilentlyContinue

name: Disable Typing & Inking Data Collection
description: Stops Windows from collecting typing patterns and inking data to send to Microsoft.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows sends keyboard typing patterns and handwriting data to improve speech/ink recognition models.
educationalContext.pros: Your typing patterns and handwriting data stay completely private.
educationalContext.cons: Personalized autocorrect and ink recognition may be less accurate.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Duplicate or alias for 'DisableHandwritingData'.
educationalContext.interactions: Prevents keylogging-style data collection.

name: Set Diagnostic Data to Minimum
description: Sets Windows diagnostic data collection to 'Required' (minimum) — the lowest level allowed.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows collects diagnostic data at Full or Required levels. This forces the minimum level.
educationalContext.pros: Minimizes telemetry sent to Microsoft to only what's required for security updates.
educationalContext.cons: Tailored experiences and optional diagnostic insights won't work.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Alias for 'DisableTelemetry' specifically targeting the '0' or '1' registry hex value for DiagTrack.
educationalContext.interactions: Requires the 'DiagTrack' service to be running to respect the flag, whereas completely disabling the service halts it entirely.

name: Disable PowerShell 7 Telemetry
description: Sets the environment variable to disable PowerShell 7+ telemetry data collection.

educationalContext.howItWorks: PowerShell 7+ sends usage telemetry unless the POWERSHELL_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT variable is set.
educationalContext.pros: No PowerShell usage data sent to Microsoft.
educationalContext.cons: None. This only affects telemetry, not functionality.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets an environment variable 'POWERSHELL_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT=1' which prevents PowerShell core from sending startup analytics to Microsoft.
educationalContext.interactions: Highly recommended for developers. Invisible to normal users.

name: Disable Cross-Device Resume
description: Disables the cross-device activity resume feature in Windows 11 24H2+.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows 11 24H2 can resume activities started on your phone or other devices. This disables it.
educationalContext.pros: No cross-device activity data shared with Microsoft servers.
educationalContext.cons: You can't resume mobile activities on your PC.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Disables the CloudStore sync mechanism that allows you to 'continue where you left off' across multiple Windows machines.
educationalContext.interactions: Redundancy check against Timeline and Clipboard sync limitations.

name: Set Unnecessary Services to Manual
description: Sets non-essential Windows services to Manual startup so they only run when needed.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Many Windows services auto-start but are rarely used. Setting them to Manual means they start on-demand.
educationalContext.pros: Faster boot, lower RAM usage, reduced background CPU activity.
educationalContext.cons: First use of some features may take a moment longer as the service starts.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Changes the startup type from 'Automatic' to 'Manual' for several non-critical background services (e.g., edgeupdate, MapsBroker). This means they will only start when explicitly called by Windows or an application, rather than idling in the background constantly.
educationalContext.interactions: A safe middle-ground compared to forcefully 'Disabling' services. Ensures features work when needed but saves RAM on boot.

execution.code: $services = @('DiagTrack','dmwappushservice','MapsBroker','lfsvc','SharedAccess','RemoteRegistry','RemoteAccess','TrkWks','WMPNetworkSvc','WSearch'); foreach ($s in $services) { if (Get-Service -Name $s -EA SilentlyContinue) { Set-Service -Name $s -StartupType Manual -EA SilentlyContinue } }; Set-Service -Name 'DiagTrack' -StartupType Disabled -EA SilentlyContinue; Stop-Service -Name 'DiagTrack' -Force -EA SilentlyContinue
execution.revertCode: $services = @('DiagTrack','dmwappushservice','MapsBroker','TrkWks','WSearch'); foreach ($s in $services) { if (Get-Service -Name $s -EA SilentlyContinue) { Set-Service -Name $s -StartupType Automatic -EA SilentlyContinue } }

name: Disable Explorer Automatic Folder Discovery
description: Stops Explorer from auto-detecting folder types (Music, Pictures, etc.) which causes slow enumeration.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Explorer scans folder contents to decide the view template (Pictures, Music, etc.). This is slow for large folders.
educationalContext.pros: Dramatically faster folder opening, especially for game install dirs and large downloads.
educationalContext.cons: Folders always use the General template. No automatic Music/Pictures column layouts.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Modifies 'HKCU\Software\Classes\Local Settings\Software\Microsoft\Windows\Shell\Bags\AllFolders\Shell' to set 'FolderType' to 'NotSpecified'. This stops Windows Explorer from automatically scanning folder contents (like pictures or videos) to determine the view layout, speeding up folder navigation.
educationalContext.interactions: Removes automatic thumbnail or media layout views for media folders. You will need to manually set the view type if you want large icons.

name: Disable Lock Screen Ads & Spotlight
description: Removes ads, tips, and Microsoft Spotlight from the lock screen.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Windows shows ads disguised as 'tips' and 'fun facts' on the lock screen. This disables all of them.
educationalContext.pros: Clean lock screen with no marketing content. Faster lock screen loading.
educationalContext.cons: You lose the rotating Bing wallpapers from Windows Spotlight.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Switches the lock screen from 'Windows Spotlight' (which downloads high-res images and ads from Bing) to 'Picture' mode, and disables the underlying ContentDeliveryManager tasks.
educationalContext.interactions: Your lock screen will statically display a single image forever, without rotating or showing tooltip facts.

execution.code: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ContentDeliveryManager' -Name 'RotatingLockScreenOverlayEnabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ContentDeliveryManager' -Name 'SubscribedContent-338387Enabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ContentDeliveryManager' -Name 'SubscribedContent-338389Enabled' -Value 0 -Type DWord -Force

name: Set DNS to Mullvad (100.64.0.4)
description: Sets DNS to Mullvad's privacy-focused DNS with ad/tracker blocking.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Mullvad DNS blocks ads, trackers, and malware at the DNS level. No logs, no data collection.
educationalContext.pros: Privacy-first DNS with built-in ad/tracker blocking. No logging policy.
educationalContext.cons: Slower than Cloudflare for raw speed. Some sites may break if ads are blocked.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets DNS to 100.64.0.4. Uses Mullvad's privacy-focused, zero-logging DNS resolvers.
educationalContext.interactions: Overrides ISP DNS. Uncensored and strongly audited for privacy.

name: Set DNS to AdGuard (94.140.14.14)
description: Sets DNS to AdGuard's DNS which blocks ads, trackers, and phishing domains.

educationalContext.howItWorks: AdGuard DNS resolves normal domains but returns NXDOMAIN for known ad/tracker/malware domains.
educationalContext.pros: System-wide ad blocking at the DNS level. Blocks trackers and phishing sites.
educationalContext.cons: Some ad-supported sites or services may not work correctly.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets DNS to 94.140.14.14 and 94.140.15.15. Routes all requests through AdGuard's DNS-level sinkhole, dropping requests to known ad and tracking domains.
educationalContext.interactions: Provides system-wide ad blocking (even inside Metro apps) without needing a browser extension.

name: Set DNS to Quad9 (9.9.9.9)
description: Sets DNS to Quad9 which provides malware blocking and privacy protection.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Quad9 uses threat intelligence from 40+ partners to block malicious domains at the DNS level.
educationalContext.pros: Strong malware domain blocking. Non-profit, privacy-focused. Swiss legal jurisdiction.
educationalContext.cons: Slightly slower than Cloudflare. Very aggressive blocking may rarely flag legitimate sites.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets DNS to 9.9.9.9 and 149.112.112.112. Quad9 cross-references queries against IBM X-Force threat intelligence to silently block malware/phishing domains at the DNS level.
educationalContext.interactions: Provides baseline system security against zero-day phishing links.

name: Set DNS to NextDNS (45.90.28.0)
description: Sets DNS to NextDNS — a customizable DNS with ad blocking, analytics, and parental controls.

educationalContext.howItWorks: NextDNS lets you customize your blocklists online. The default server provides basic protection.
educationalContext.pros: Highly customizable via nextdns.io dashboard. Excellent analytics and logging controls.
educationalContext.cons: Free tier limited to 300K queries/month. Full customization requires creating a NextDNS account.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets DNS to 45.90.28.0 and 45.90.30.0. NextDNS is a highly configurable cloud firewall.
educationalContext.interactions: Using the raw IPs provides basic filtering, but to get full customization (custom blocklists), you need their dedicated client or DoH profile.

name: Set DNS to OpenDNS / Cisco Umbrella (208.67.222.222)
description: Sets DNS to Cisco's OpenDNS with phishing/botnet protection and content filtering.

educationalContext.howItWorks: OpenDNS (now Cisco Umbrella) provides DNS-level security with enterprise-grade threat detection.
educationalContext.pros: Excellent phishing and botnet protection. Optional content filtering via OpenDNS account.
educationalContext.cons: Owned by Cisco — may log queries for analytics. Slightly slower in some regions.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets DNS to 208.67.222.222 and 208.67.220.220. A venerable, highly reliable DNS service operated by Cisco.
educationalContext.interactions: Faster than ISP DNS, includes basic anti-phishing.

name: Set DNS to Control D (76.76.2.0)
description: Sets DNS to Control D — a customizable DNS with ad blocking and geo-unblocking.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Control D provides DNS-level ad blocking and can unblock geo-restricted content.
educationalContext.pros: Ad blocking + geo-unblocking in one DNS. Very fast resolver infrastructure.
educationalContext.cons: Full features require a paid account. Free tier is basic DNS resolution.
educationalContext.interactions: A modern alternative to NextDNS/AdGuard with extremely fast anycast routing.

name: Set DNS to Google (8.8.8.8)
description: Sets DNS to Google Public DNS — the most widely used public DNS resolver.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Google Public DNS is a fast, reliable DNS resolver. No ad blocking or filtering.
educationalContext.pros: Extremely fast and reliable. Massive global infrastructure with low latency.
educationalContext.cons: Google logs DNS queries (anonymized after 48h). No ad/tracker blocking.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets DNS to 8.8.8.8 and 8.8.4.4. The most widespread public DNS.
educationalContext.interactions: Fastest sheer resolution time globally, but queries are logged by Google.

name: Reset DNS to ISP Default (DHCP)
description: Resets DNS back to your ISP's default DNS servers via DHCP.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Removes any manually set DNS servers and lets your router/ISP assign DNS automatically via DHCP.
educationalContext.pros: Returns to default network configuration. Useful if a custom DNS is causing issues.
educationalContext.cons: Your ISP can see and log all your DNS queries. Usually slower than public DNS.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Removes manual DNS server assignments, reverting the adapter back to dynamic (DHCP) from your router.
educationalContext.interactions: Necessary to revert any connection issues caused by a custom DNS going offline.

name: Disable Action Center
description: Removes the Action Center and notifications sidebar.

educationalContext.howItWorks: Sets DisableNotificationCenter in Group Policy.
educationalContext.pros: Removes notification distractions and frees up taskbar space.
educationalContext.cons: You will no longer receive any Windows notifications.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Duplicate or alias for 'DisableNotificationCenter'.
educationalContext.interactions: Same caveats apply.

execution.code: if (!(Test-Path 'HKCU:\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer')) { New-Item -Path 'HKCU:\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer' -Force | Out-Null }; Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKCU:\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer' -Name 'DisableNotificationCenter' -Value 1 -Type DWord -Force; Stop-Process -Name explorer -Force -EA SilentlyContinue

name: Disable Virtualization-Based Security (VBS)
description: Disables VBS to recover 5–15% GPU/CPU performance lost to hypervisor overhead. Requires reboot.

educationalContext.howItWorks: VBS uses Windows Hypervisor to create an isolated virtual environment protecting kernel memory. It runs even on non-Hyper-V systems and incurs a measurable performance cost.
educationalContext.pros: Recovers 5–15% GPU performance in games; reduces CPU overhead; useful on older hardware.
educationalContext.cons: Removes kernel-level memory isolation. Windows Defender Credential Guard will be disabled. Increases risk from kernel exploits.
educationalContext.expertDetails: Sets the bootloader entry 'hypervisorlaunchtype' to 'off' via bcdedit. VBS is enabled by default on Windows 11. Disabling it also disables HVCI (Memory Integrity) since that feature depends on VBS. Reboot is required for changes to take effect.
